docs.reply: Hello Kenneth,      ​Sorry for my late response we are experiencing a heavy load of request right now. We are still catching up. In the log you shared me I can see some failures with the NG buffer... let me elaborate a bit more.     In the NG Buffer there are 2 registers associated with each Slot:  ​1- First one has the "PCBGUID" for us to know which PCB is loaded there  2-Status of the Inspection, here are the posible states:       ​The "Grey" state that you are reporting us is the last state (0600) which means that there is value for status registered but no value for the first register (PCBGUI)  If we look at the communication between our machine and the NG_Buffer we can see that NG_Buffer does not send the last "ACK" command (Yellow)      Expected sequence:      Logs found:           Después de que se termina la inspección (406) la comunicación con el buffer comienza y se le envía los datos al buffer (411 & 413) sin embargo nos quedamos esperando a que el Buffer active el registro correspondiente al ACK final, eventualmente después de esperar se llega el timeout (Rojo linea 455) y da como resultado el slot en Gris. Favor de reenviarle a su proveedor de NG Buffer esta información.      Solo como referencia dejare un flujo de como sucede normalmente, a diferencia de el anterior donde ocurrió el error aquí si llego el ACK final (amarillo)             Best regards

docs.cust: Dears, I’m Luciano, a member of Simplex Technology – Tecnolab, the Italian distributor for Koh Young. Below are a few words to explain how the Koh Young machines are configured at Pittway – Honeywell...

docs.reply: Hi Luciano,  Currently, CFX is using only one handle (in BRM line 1), That's why Line 1 is working correctly.   You can use the following setup and configure the Review stations to work with each BRM installed in the AOI machines:    LINE 1 – REVIEW 1    LINE 2 – REVIEW 2    LINE 3 – REVIEW 3             Configuring the Review Stations (1, 2, and 3)  After completing the initial setup, follow these steps to configure each Review Station (1, 2, and 3) to work with the corresponding production line. Since the SQL query is the same across all three lines, the process will be consistent for each station.    Open the configuration interface of Review Station.    Open the Review>Line interface.    Filter the Machine:    Check the "Line Filter" checkbox to enable the filter.      Fill in the Machine ID and IP:    In the Machine ID field, enter the identifier for the machine associated with the production line.    In the Machine IP field, enter the IP address of the machine.      Save the Configuration:    Click on the Add button to register the machine.    Then, click OK to save the settings.      Repeat this process for all three Review Stations, ensuring each one is correctly configured for its respective production line.      After following this steps, you should be able to see the accurate yield percentage reflected in the charts.  If you need any assistance with the configuration process or encounter any issues, please don't hesitate to let me know.   I look forward to your reply.     Best regards

docs.reply: Hello Tofani,      We’re not entirely sure how your previous setup was configured, but we do have the installation manual that was used last year. That manual doesn’t account for any special scenarios or the use of a single SQL server, nor does it cover the specific requirements you’re now mentioning.  We’re doing our best to adapt your setup as requested, but it's important to understand that connecting all review stations to a single SQL—while it does simplify the process by allowing any station to be used—also introduces complexity in data handling. Specifically, we lose the ability to identify which AOI processed each PCB.  There are tools available (as mentioned in previous emails) that can handle this setup by filtering PCBs based on specific IPs, but we understand this approach isn’t feasible for your case.  Given the constraints, the solution we’ve proposed is the only viable one we’ve identified that supports a single SQL setup.     Best regards

docs.reply: Hola Jesus,      Creo que no comprendo a que te refieres con plan B te comparto el root cause que le compartí a Kenneth que describe el origen del problema.      In the NG Buffer there are 2 registers associated with each Slot:  ​1- First one has the "PCBGUID" for us to know which PCB is loaded there  2-Status of the Inspection, here are the posible states:       ​The "Grey" state that you are reporting us is the last state (0600) which means that there is value for status registered but no value for the first register (PCBGUI)  If we look at the communication between our machine and the NG_Buffer we can see that NG_Buffer does not send the last "ACK" command (Yellow)      Expected sequence:      Logs found:           Después de que se termina la inspección (406) la comunicación con el buffer comienza y se le envía los datos al buffer (411 & 413) sin embargo nos quedamos esperando a que el Buffer active el registro correspondiente al ACK final, eventualmente después de esperar se llega el timeout (Rojo linea 455) y da como resultado el slot en Gris. Favor de reenviarle a su proveedor de NG Buffer esta información.      Solo como referencia dejare un flujo de como sucede normalmente, a diferencia de el anterior donde ocurrió el error aquí si llego el ACK final (amarillo)       Cualquier duda quedo al pendiente de sus comentarios.      Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Que tal Christian,      Esto que comentas si es posible y hay dos caminos:     Activando el Save Good Result + Details: El tipo de inspección que se esta usando en las imágenes que muestras es la de OCV /OCR sin referencia por lo que al no tener una referencia no nos es posible determinar si es buena o mala únicamente guardamos el dato leído y asumimos este es el correcto. Por defecto el equipo solo guarda las inspecciones NG por lo que esta inspección de OCR/OCV no sera guardada ya que siempre es buena. Probablemente lo que te haga falta habilitar adicional a las opciones que mencionas sea esta de aca:  Esta opción si recuerdo bien te daría el valor sin necesidad de modificación o si se necesita seria algo menor. Solo que te dará los valores por separados no los unirá. Lo único a considerar es que habilitar esta opción hara que tu incremento en espacio crezca exponencialmente y el disco duro con el que viene el equipo no sera suficiente habrá que estarlo depurando constantemente y así mismo el guardado de información tardara mas lo cual incrementa el cycle time. Si tu estas de acuerdo con estas limitantes podemos irnos por esta opción pero creo mas factible la #2    A través de un desarrollo y utilizando la herramienta de coverage report + la opción de arriba podemos entregarte exactamente lol que estas buscando. Ambas lecturas unificadas y si gbustas tambien podemos utilizar el valor para el nombre del archivo. En esta opcion no es necesario activar los good results + detils por lo que no tendras los problemas descritos arriba sin embargo conlleva un costo.      Quedo al pendiente de tus comentarios para saber como te gustaría proseguir.      Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hello Steven,      BRM software always preserve the settings the user select as long as they click on "SAVE" icon.      When User do a change a triangle icon (Yellow) is placed next to the option to depict that setting has change its value and is still not saved.   If the customer have the triangle icon then they must click on both save icons (Red) for BRM to save the value, once this is made even after a reboot the setting will hold its value.      If they do these changes and still face the same issue please tell them to open a ticket so we can assist them.              Best regards

docs.reply: Hola Jorge,     Muchas gracias por atender mi llamada. En ella, pudimos confirmar el requerimiento, el cual es el siguiente:     "Unity pudiera responder con False en el campo item1 de su respuesta. De momento, la lógica dejará avanzar la inspección siempre y cuando uno de los paneles tenga valor en True. El cambio que necesitan es que, adicional a este campo de item1, revisemos el texto de item2, donde se encuentra la descripción de por qué es PASS o FAIL. Desean que, si en ese texto el fallo es de SPI, se detenga la inspección. Es decir, tener la capacidad de hacer secuencia A o secuencia B dependiendo de lo que se encuentre en el texto del campo item2."     Dicho esto, te comparto la cotización para dicho desarrollo. Este, al ser un nuevo requerimiento adicional a los requerimientos iniciales, conlleva un rediseño en la lógica que habíamos creado inicialmente. Normalmente, cuando se nos piden ajustes sencillos, sin problema podemos ayudar, como fue el caso de tu anterior ticket, donde solicitaste un ajuste para la lógica que sigue la SPI. Sin embargo, en este caso sí se requiere un ajuste mayor, y por lo tanto, el cobro correspondiente. Después de platicarlo con nuestro equipo de ventas, autorizaron un descuento, el cual podrás ver reflejado dentro de la cotización.     Quedamos atentos a tus comentarios para definir los siguientes pasos.     Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hello Paul,     During todays meeting the we reviewed the issue where a notification was poping up to the right of the screen. The review settup was verified, every Review is pointing to the correct machine. The socket was resset and we didnt see the issue again during the call. If it happens again please generate an issue report and reset the socket.     Here is the update for the tracked points:   1. We have solved the Fail Code issue and can close that ticket if there is  one.  We agreed to update KBR on next release. ​ KBR uses the correct customer codes   2. The warning that was popping up was solved in the 6.1 update. Warning pop up not showing up now   3. The file output from BRM that saved the QST files was modified to save the data as customer/assembly/rev/side did not need to be changed and caused issues it just needs to put the qst files to a single place. D:\KohYoung\Plexus\Temp\QST QST Files generated in the right location  4. We want to get the output from the GUI the 'Coverage report and Image' file to save as customer/assembly/rev/side/sn as the SPI is setup to do now.  **NEW  CSV is being exported to the correct location. Logs were gathered and development will continue for the image to be saved on the job related location and with the name Timestamp_SN.  5. Save the coverage report file with SN in title instead of PCBGUID  I am not sure whey we need the PCBGUID option but we prefer to have sn in title. Or if we add to file we can append it afte the fact.  CSV export is named Timestamp_SN       Best regards

docs.reply: Hello Tom,      Nice to meet you! My name is Luis Rivera, MES Leader at Koh Young America. I can assist you with this request. Unfortunately, my team and I are based in Mexico, so I believe it would be best to first try to define this request through email/meetings before considering an on-site visit, in order to expedite the solution.     I looked at our records, and it seems there have been no previous developments for your site, so I'm assuming this is your first request regarding MES connectivity.     Based on your initial message, I believe you are looking for a file-based system where our software creates files after every inspection in a specific path for your system to parse and use. If this is the case, please refer to the attached document, which provides a detailed description of our file-based standard. In the document, you’ll find specifics such as the supported formats (CSV/XML), the file name structure we follow, and the details of the actual content, including a description of the NG components from the inspection.     A few things to consider: this standard is FOC (Free of Charge) if you decide to use it as-is. However, if you decide to make any major adjustments to the solution, there may be an associated development fee. I am sharing this standard because I believe it aligns with your needs, but please note that the tool we use to create this solution also allows us to develop various other interfaces, such as Web Services using REST API, direct SQL using stored procedures, and industry standards like CFX, iTAC, SECS/GEM. If there is another interface you're looking for that I haven't mentioned, please know that we are open to developing it.     Also, can you help me clarify if you are looking to have this solution in in SPI or AOI systems? I'll be waiting for your comments to determine if this standard fulfills your needs and to define the next steps.     Best regards

docs.reply: Hello Paul,      Please follow these intructions to add new defect to your equipments.     In the review Station Software please go to SETUP tab and select the option Custom Defect Editor. Inside use the + icon to add a new defect.        ​      Please don't change the code that is generated only change the name of the new defect code. After this is done please click save and restart the Review GUI software to reflect new changes.   The previous steps were just to add the defect to the review Station, now we must configure BRM to include this new defect as well. Inside BRM please go to the tab   BRM>Codes>AOI  and click the button "Sync with review defect code" after this if new defect is not reflected on the defect grid in BRM please try rebooting BRM and checking again.                    Please   Best regards

docs.reply: Hola Houser, Jonathan.     During the remote session, we successfully established a proper connection between the Buffer and the AOI machine. We noticed two hubs: the first one with RS485 connections and the second one with RS232. In this case, the Buffer needed to be properly connected to the second hub. We changed the serial ports from 6-7 to 14-15 and established a connection only with port 15. We also ran tests to verify the correct data exchange between the Buffer and the BRM. We are still waiting to verify if the other COM port is required to work properly with the PLC​ or if it has a physical problem with the cable.   Please don’t hesitate to reach out if you encounter any issues or have any questions.    Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hello Jonathan,      Thanks for your insights they are really helpful. Let me elaborate on all your comments as you raised some very good points here:      3. I know Koh Young Korea and DJK in Korea worked together in the past to get these set up, but I can’t really say whether having them look at it together is a viable option. If you wanted to try that route, I could send the PLC program to DJK to examine in Korea and have them discuss with Koh Young Korea, but I don’t know how closely you guys work with the Korean branch of Koh Young or if that seems like a realistic option.  This has already happened, not now but it has happened, the tool we use (BRM) force us to select a PLC manufacture depending on which one is the Buffer using. (Panasonic, Omron, Mitsubishi) the result of this collaboration were this plugins that ideally each PLC brand uses. If I'm not wrong the Communication protocol is the same across these 3 brands and the only difference is the memory map addresses for each PLC brand.     The communication method I know has 2 registers associated to each slot in the buffer:     1- First is the Identificator Register that is being used to hold the PCBGUID of the inspection, this way when user select a slot we know which PCB is inside  2-Second is the Status Registe that is being used to hold the Status of the PCB inside. (NG Pending, NG confirmed, Good, etc.)           What I saw on the test we did last time was that only the second register (status) had value, with no ID on the other register. It had default value of 00000000. The register that needs to change depends on which slot does the Buffer stores the Slot. In the experiment we did last time the missing register was this one (FRONT_RACK_PCBID_3) The first highlighted blue one. In there you can see the starting Number of the register. What I suggest we do is set a working session like the previous one to do the same experiment with the working machine. we send a NG board collect the logs, take a screenshot of the memory map once the board is inside and then repeat on the non working machine to create a comparison that can be shared with the Korean programmers.   ​   The only reason I don't want to go on with your first choice is because I'm not a PLC programmer I just know what registers should have value so not sure if that will be good enough to help you do the change.      Here is the sequence that should happen:    ​      The problem is that I saw receive properly both ACK from Buffer this confirm that PCBID was set but I couldn't see any value. Please disregard the register address in the diagram they assume is Panasonic PLC use the ones in the first image.      Best regards

docs.reply: Hola equipo Skyworks,      Muchas gracias por su interés. CFX es un estándar open-source promovido por IPC con una alta tasa de adopción en la Industria 4.0 basado en el protocolo AMQP que evita la perdida de datos y utiliza el formato JSON con un mecanismo de compresión GZIP para mayor eficiencia en el envío de datos. Para más detalles, les recomiendo visiten los siguientes sitios y cualquier duda quedamos a la orden.      Artículo donde pueden encontrar los beneficios y aplicaciones de CFX: https://www.ipc.org/about-ipc-cfx-and-your-path-ipc-cfx-success  Equipos y marcas que están oficialmente certificados por IPC: https://certification.connectedfactoryexchange.com/certification-directory  Guía oficial para integrar su sistema de MES/SAP con CFX: https://shop.ipc.org/general-electronics/standards/2591-3-0v1-3-english  Por último, aquí pueden descargar el Software Development Kit (SDK) sin costo alguno: http://www.connectedfactoryexchange.com/cfxdemo/sdk/html/cfb57aac-b696-4c6c-b94b-f034b37daf26.htm      En relación a los costos, pueden adquirir nuestro modulo de CFX por $2,200 USD por máquina, $10K USD por tipo de máquina (SPI o AOI), o bien $15K USD por planta (SPI y AOI). Los últimos dos planes cubren todas las máquinas actuales y futuras compras incluyendo el soporte y actualizaciones de CFX por los próximos 5 años. Quedamos al pendiente de sus comentarios en caso de que tengan alguna duda.      Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: ​Hello Brett,     Apologies for the delay. We've experienced a higher volume of incoming tickets and requests than usual.   The update would only be required for the SPI machines. The latest versions require KY_KSMART_BRM_Setup_x64_2.7.1.4.exe to be installed on the machine unlike Flex_Austin_SPI_V2.2.kbr which requires KY_KSMART_BRM_Setup_x64_2.6.0.17_Release.exe. The change required to fix the reported problem would be developed and included in a new version [P2.7.1.4]Flex_Austin_SPI_V2.7.1.kbr.      The changes that might have a bigger impact are related to Flex Flow and File Export. Could you please provide a screenshot of the BRM Customer Properties screen so we can verify which file export is currently in use and determine if the changes will affect it? Thank you. docs.reply: Hello Tim,      The issue we’re currently facing with enabling the "Repair Later" feature is not related to the version of the GUI software.  The CFX feature requires the BRM to be active on the AOI to generate the messages properly.   The "Repair Later" feature can still run, but in this case, a pop-up menu will appear where BRM is installed so the operator can interact with it. This is why we need BRM installed on the Review Station. If we try using the tool now, it will work, but instead of displaying the pop-up menu on the Review, it will show up on the AOI where BRM is installed.  That being said, please let me know if you would like more details on what’s needed to upgrade to version 2.9. I can loop in one of my Help Desk colleagues, as they are more skilled in this area than I am and they for sure have all the details.      Best regards

docs.reply: Hello Samantha,     Yes, Koh Young does support the HERMES standard. However, based on my understanding of your initial request, it seems that this may not be suitable. The HERMES standard is primarily designed for machine-to-machine (M2M) communication (horizontal), whereas your request appears to involve pushing inspection data to your MES system, which involves vertical communication. For this purpose, I would recommend using the CFX protocol instead.      Best regards

docs.reply: Hola Mario Hernandez.     Buen día, podrías compartirnos por favor el UUID de esta maquina?      Abrir la terminal (CMD): Presiona las teclas Windows + R, escribe cmd y haz clic en Aceptar.    Escribir el comando: Copia y pega el siguiente comando en la terminal y presiona Enter: wmic path win32_computersystemproduct get uuid     Ver el UUID: Después de ejecutar el comando, verás una línea con la etiqueta UUID y el valor correspondiente. Ese es el identificador único de la máquina.    Copiar el UUID: Selecciona el UUID con el mouse, haz clic derecho y elige Copiar. También puedes presionar Ctrl + C después de seleccionarlo.     Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hello Ross,     Nice to meet you! My name is Luis Rivera, and I'm the MES team leader here at Koh Young America. Our tool is designed to provide per-inspection data through exported files, web services, SQL stored procedures, CFX, and more. I'll provide two of our standard solutions, which offer plenty of data, but please keep in mind that we are open to developing a more customized solution if these two don't fully meet your requirements. docs.reply: Hello       Could you confirm whether the solutions you’re offering—exported files, web services, SQL stored procedures, and CFX—are included as part of our existing KSmart setup, or if they come at an additional cost? Only the two solutions I shared in my last message are FOC (Free of charge) all the other solutions will require a license fee to activate them. Please let me know if the solutions I provided cover your needs so we can proceed to develop it.   In addition to capturing job-related data from SPI and AOI, we also require data from our Pick & Place machines. Could you let us know if this data is available through the same integration methods, and if so, which tables or endpoints would provide it? Hello Unfortunately Koh Young does not have any Pick & Place machines so it is hard to retrieve data from machines that we do not own. Not sure which type of data are you looking to have? There are a few features that have communication with Pick and Place machines called KPO Mounter which is like a closed loop feature to adjust itself. This is only available on FUJI & ASM equipment. Is this what you are talking about? If not please provide as much detail as possible to see if we can help you in this matter.      This week I'll be attending APEX trade show so there will be delay in my responses. Appreciate understanding.      Best regards

docs.reply: Hello Tim,     Thank you for the file with the expected behavior of the development. We have received the requirements and are working on the implementation. We have some comments:     1) Shall - Use timing to ensure we have not missed the scan. The scan time should be very close to the trigger inspection inside the system.    For the first inspection, the development will store the timestamp from the Barcode.txt. On the next inspection, previous timestamp will be compared against the current timestamp.  If the timestamps are different, the barcode is assigned and the "previous timestamp" is updated.  If the timestamps are the same, which means there is not a new barcode entry. Should the machine stop the inspection?     2) Shall - Maintain all current KBR functionality and add this as an option to the KBR.    This feature will be a separate entry in the Customer Property tab without interfering on the current development.   3) Shall - The regular hand-scan process needs to be working in parallel with the inline scanner process. (backup if a bottom read can not be performed) we would just move the in-line scanner to the back    As a separate feature it will have its own Use checkbox.   ​Is this scenario for whenever the KYConfig and Settings are configured for In Line scanning? ​   ​Should this feature work only when there is not a Barcode read configured?      4) Shall - If the CamBarcode turned on the in-line will not work.    As a separate feature it will have its own Use checkbox.   If the feature is enabled and the CamBarcode is enabled, should CamBarcode take over the barcode assignment?     5) Shall - When changing the Job the barcode file will be renamed, if exists, to the Job prior to load with a timestamp added.    The file will be renamed to JOBNAME_TIMESTAMP.txt   Best regards

docs.reply: Hello Alonso,     Thanks for receiving me yesterday. We were able to test the two PCBs you had:      First PCB: With this board, we consistently encountered the error: "Index was out of range. Must be non-negative and less than the size of the collection. Parameter name: index" This has been the most confusing issue in this implementation, as the error message provides very little detail about the root cause.     Second PCB: Testing with this board was extremely promising. We're not exactly sure what internal differences exist between the two boards, but with this one, we successfully tested both communication methods (File-Based and API-Based). From the experiments we ran, we were able to successfully retrieve the serial numbers using the new DLL function: UnitsAreVerifiedByPanel.        There are a couple of things I'd like to ask the developers :  1-Is there a debug log file of your DLL? This in order to try to understand the issue described in bullet #1  2-Can they confirm whether the version we are currently using is the latest, or if there’s a more recent one available?     ​      Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hello Adriana,     Thank you for the call today. We were able to go thru the open points for AOI and SPI. Here is an update.   AOI - CFX Customer Defect Code  The problem where the custom defects were not being sent was solved by setting the Defect Codes as Customer within the IPC-CFX configuration window.     AOI - CFX UnitsInspected Defect structure  The dashboard is currently using a combination of DefectCode  and DefectName or DefectCategory from the CFX message. The values of these fields are built using the following values taken from BRM > Codes > AOI > Defect.  DefectCode - CustomerCode  DefectCategory - CustomerName  DefectName - CustomerName    To make sure that the dashboard shows the expected value which is the DefectCode only, please delete the CustomerName values from the table. This approach was tested and the QST file is not being affected by the change.     ​SPI licenses  New .lic file was shared using kiteworks for the 10 requested machines    SPI - CFX Dashboard Incorrect Panel Count  ARCH has requested additional logs from he machine that is working as expected. For us to be able to follow up on their comments, please enable Verbose in the SPI using the IPC-CFX configuration window            Best regards

docs.reply: Hello Adriana,     Thank you for the information for the version that has been stabilized by KYE.      The Can not read inspection data. Error message is triggered by the KY-MES software attempting to access an  inspection result before it has been properly saved by the SPI GUI. Please set the KY-MES trigger Delay (ms) value to 1000 which can be found under BRM > Plugins > Message Forwarding   We have received feedback from HQ regarding the KYWholeBoard image name and the stroke error. Both issues share the same root cause: The panel barcodes assigned by the Array Parser are replacing the Master Barcode. docs.reply: Hola, Alan Navarro Z.    Con respecto a lo que discutimos en la reunión pasada, te agradezco por compartir los requerimientos. Después de revisar los puntos mencionados, hay algunas consideraciones que me gustaría aclarar sobre la factibilidad del desarrollo:    Tras la clasificación en Review, el único cambio que se realiza es la actualización de la información en las aplicaciones internas (SPC/KSMART Server).    AOI y señal OK/NG al buffer: No es viable, ya que las señales SMEMA solo aplican a la tablilla dentro del equipo, no a las que ya han salido.    Comunicación con NG Buffers: Actualmente, solo se soporta comunicación mediante el protocolo MEWTOCOL.    Dado lo anterior, el desarrollo en su forma actual no es factible. Sin embargo, adjunto un documento con información sobre el protocolo MEWTOCOL, el cual podría ayudar a cumplir con los requerimientos solicitados.  Quedo atento a cualquier duda o comentario. ​ Saludos cordiales

Hola Sandra,     A partir de la información proporcionada, se puede integrar la función de Review Feedback. Esta función mantiene la tablilla dentro de la máquina en espera de su clasificación. Una vez evaluada, la tablilla sale de la máquina con el resultado final.   En aquellos casos en los que la clasificación debe realizarse fuera de la máquina, es necesario contar con un NG buffer posterior al AOI. Este buffer se comunica con el BRM a través de protocolos de comunicación como Metwocool y en la salida del buffer es dónde las tablillas tienen el resultado dado en la Review.          Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hello Chang,      Correct the first document describes the expected flows for each scneario (Review Pass, Review NG, etc) however the commands to actually read/write memory values of the PLC is described in the documentation provided by Panasonic (send document) since you told me you were using panasonic PLC I assume that this was already taken into consideration.      Does the AOI both send and receive signals from the conveyor PLC?'  We are reading/writing regiters in the PLC Memory map, we do not receive any signal directly. For example:   The arrows here from AOI to PLC show that we (Koh Young) are going to write the value of 1 in Register 701 And when the arrow is from PLC to AOI it shows that we are going to be "polling" that register to look for a change, once you internally do that change in that register we confirm that the sequence went as expected.     Is the AOI communication flow aligned with the diagram provided earlier(showing as below), including PASS/NG processes? Are the PLC addresses consistent with those marked in the diagram?  Yes those are the registers, I'll attach the registers I'm using in this message, there you can see the addresses.     Does review station communicate with conveyor using RS232 communication protocol?  Correct RS232        There is a simplified version of communication that only sends ASCII commands, there is no need to read/write any PLC memory addresses. I'll attach it as reference.       Best regards

docs.reply: Hola Jorge,     Se utiliza el mismo desarrollo, con diferentes configuraciones según el caso. Para habilitar la comunicación con un NG Buffer, lo que hacemos es activar los plugins NG Buffer y Panasonic PLC (en el caso de Mewtocool). Las diferencias entre configuraciones se encuentran únicamente en las pestañas y se ajustan de acuerdo a las caracteristicas del buffer, por lo que no se requiere desarrollo adicional.  Para realizar estas configuraciones, se pueden programar sesiones de trabajo con la participación del proveedor o del programador del PLC, según sea necesario.    Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hello David,  I've forwarded this request to our official support email. There's nothing else you need to do at the moment — just make sure to CC kya_support@kohyoung.com in your future replies. Regarding the errors you mentioned:  First Issue: The empty field in the first line is most likely due to the absence of a master barcode on the PCB.   Second Issue: In this case, it's the opposite — the master barcode is present on the PCB, but the panel barcode (child) is missing, which leaves the field empty. We can definitely adjust the logic so that if the PCB only has one panel, the master barcode is used instead. This will prevent the field from being empty.  Third Issue: A status of "Fail" typically means the barcode was not read properly. This could be due to a narrow FOV box or lighting conditions. Adjusting these settings may help recover the barcode correctly.  Could you please share the issue report with me? Knowing which BRM the customer is using at their facility will help us determine whether any changes are necessary, I'll attach a manual to retrieve this log.   Best regards

docs.reply: Hello Brent/David,     Thank you for the file. I have now confirmed the comments by Luis Rivera and identified the development being used on site.      Changes can be made for First and Second issue. We can update the export to fit your requirements and cover these differences in programing. Please let us know how would you like the changes to be implemented.  Third issue is related to programing, the barcode inspection needs to be adjusted.      First Issue: The empty field in the first line is due to the absence of a master barcode on the PCB. We can make the change to use the first panel (child) barcode at the top of the file to avoid leaving it empty.     Second Issue: In this case, it's the opposite — the master barcode is present on the PCB, but the panel barcode (child) is missing, which leaves the field empty. We can definitely adjust the logic so that if the PCB only has one panel, the master barcode is used instead. This will prevent the field from being empty.  Third Issue: A status of "Fail" typically means the barcode was not read properly. This could be due to a narrow FOV box or lighting conditions. Adjusting these settings may help recover the barcode correctly.     Best regards

Revisando los logs que pertenecen a la inspección de la tablilla con serial  P1851385-01-A:SC7R25119M60201, se identifica que continuó subiendo resultados para los paneles 9-14 con éxito. docs.reply: Hola Alejandro,     Las configuraciones para la secciones:     Code - ​Valores para manejar Custom Defects. En caso de que no manejen este tipo de defectos, no es necesario hacer modificaciones.     MES I/F - Configuración requerida para poder realizar la conexión con su web service.   Line ID : Identificador de la línea.  Use : Habilitar o deshabilitar el Web Service.  Lane : Seleccionar el Lane que reporta al Web Service.  Events:  Seleccionar los Eventos de la máquina que se reportan al Web Service  Address: URI de su Web Service  Interlocking: Habilitar o deshabilitar el detener la máquina a partir de la respuesta de su Web Service       Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hola, Martin.  Disculpa por la demora. Para realizar este tipo de exportación de archivos con el fin de llevar registros de la máquina, recomendamos hacerlo a través de un archivo .TXT. Cada que una tablilla pase por la máquina, podemos generar un archivo individual o escribir toda la información en un solo archivo acumulativo.  También recomendamos hacerlo a través de APIs, ya que es una alternativa más robusta y flexible. Sin embargo, en este caso necesitaríamos contar con toda la documentación de sus APIs o los requerimientos específicos para poder realizar una cotización adecuada.  de momento les hago llegar los costos por el archivo TXT con la información requerida.  AMOUNT: $3,000 Item: MES Software Q'TY: 1 PRODUCT: TXT file with machine data U/PRICE: $3,000 AMOUNT: $3,000    Si requieren la cotización formal, háganmelo saber para hacérselas llegar.  Saludos!!   Alexis Álvarez  MES Developer.  Koh Young America

docs.reply: Hola, Alonso Madrigal.    Para la implementación de ASM Process Expert, es necesario actualizar a la última versión de KBR y BRM, en donde se agregó esta funcionalidad.    Para SPI, se están manteniendo dos ramas de BRM:    V1.10.X o anteriores: Para máquinas donde no se requiere Process Expert. En este desarrollo, la licencia está integrada en el KBR y se requiere una actualización cada vez que se añade una nueva licencia de máquina.      V2.0.X o posteriores: Para máquinas donde se requiere Process Expert. En este desarrollo, la licencia es un archivo separado (.lic) que se carga utilizando las opciones de BRM.      Por favor, confirma cuál es la versión de SPI KBR esperada para Malasia, para que podamos proporcionar el software y las licencias correctas para los sitios (Kite works, archivo adjunto o SharePoint).  Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hola , Edgar. Respecto al NG gris del buffer es muy probable que esta sea la causa raíz:  Por cada slot necesitamos 2 registros:     1- FRONT_RACK_PCBID_#   2- FRONT_RACK_PCBSTATUS_#        El primero es un identificador para nosotros poder saber a que inspeccion hacer referencia con esa tablilla y el segundo se usa para saber el estatus de la tablilla, en los slots 1, 2 y 4 unicamente tenemos status (Unknown machine NG) ya que no tenemos identificador. El PLC no está acomodando el valor en el registro correspondiente.  hay que involucrar al proveedor del buffer para verificar estos datos.        Para confirmar que este sea el error ¿Podrías compartirnos el issue report de la maquina que tuvo esta falla desde BRM?   en estos logs también revisaré el tema de los XML que no se generaron   Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hola Gabriel,     Actualmente, el desarrollo de Mirgor guarda el valor de esos campos (FIS) por programa. Es decir, si cambian de modelo y este ya había corrido anteriormente, se usarán los datos previamente guardados. Por lo tanto, si la IP del servidor cambió, es muy probable que los valores guardados todavía contengan la IP del servidor antiguo.  Sin embargo, si ya se ajustó el dato y, al cargar de nuevo el programa, se desconfigura otra vez, entonces sí podría tratarse de un issue real.     ¿Me podrías ayudar a hacer la siguiente prueba, por favor?    Cargar el programa    Ajustar los valores correctos en BRM    Guardar los valores en BRM    Volver a cargar el Job    Si después de estos pasos los valores se desconfiguran nuevamente, necesitaré conectarme al equipo. Quedo al pendiente de sus comentarios.        Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hello, Tristan,  Thank you for reaching out. Below are the responses to your questions:    Board-specific CFX messages: By default, the CFX messages generated are machine-specific, but you can include board-level details such as serial numbers, potential defects, and more, by enabling and properly configuring the corresponding options in the system. Please ensure that the following parameters are enabled to capture detailed board information.  CFX.Production.TestAndInspection.UnitsInspected ​   For more information on all the supported CFX messages and their structure, please refer to the official documentation here: https://www.connectedfactoryexchange.com/CFXDemo/sdk/html/N_CFX_Production.htm      Missing message types (e.g., CFX.Production.WorkStarted): Please verify that the system is configured to emit all expected CFX messages. Some message types may depend on specific trigger conditions or machine states. If CFX.Production.WorkStarted is not being sent while CFX.Production.WorkStageCompleted is, it could be due to configuration or workflow setup. We recommend reviewing the production process logic to ensure the correct conditions are met for all message types to be emitted.       Sending test CFX messages manually: Unfortunately, the BRM plugin does not currently support sending manual or simulated CFX messages. Messages can only be sent as a result of real production events.    Please let us know if you'd like help reviewing your configuration or exploring other options.   Best regards

docs.reply: Hello Bill,      There are some options that can be used to stop multiple defects to be auto-rejected. In AOI please enable the following option, this will force the operator to classify all defects and only then they will be able to classify the whole board but since all defects were already classified they won't all be the same and each will have the value that the operator gave to it. This option is inside      ReviewConfig:       We have two options     1)Check : PCB level Pass/NG button will be activated when all items in the list have been viewed by the user (Not verified as Pass/NG). This was a request from a customer where they only wanted to verify the NG components and skip verification of Good components to save time. Viewed items will be highlighted in light yellow as seen below      2) Wait all: PCB Pass/NG button will be activated according to the component verification result Board Pass will be enabled when all defects are verified as Pass Board NG will be enabled when all defect are NOT verified as Pass.       Best regards

docs.reply: Hello Bill,      Unfortunately there is no skip button however there is a way to achieve similar behavior it just require some additional steps.  In the very bottom of the Review GUI you will find the "Search" section please click on it to expand and select "Manual Clear" option:  A new window will be opened with all pending PCB to classify, if you want to "Skip" just select it and click "Clear" button, once this is made these PCB will no longer appear on the Review Station             Best regards

docs.reply: Hey Benjamin,  got it! Sorry about the lane confusion  it's messy because internally the GUI calls "Lane 0" and "Lane 1", but we all know them as Lane 1 and Lane 2.   About the iTAC thing: 1.- What's weird is sometimes the TOP lane misses uploads, but then we get duplicates on others.  2.- From what I can tell, it seems like the GUI occasionally "messes with" the MasterBarcode internally, which makes BRM send weird stuff to iTAC. So iTAC shows no errors, but really the GUI isn't feeding it the right data.  3.- I'll check the logs you shared on SharePoint to see if we can spot a pattern. Sometimes this only happens with certain models or at specific times (have you noticed any particular triggers?).  If you spot anything else odd or have ideas, let me know. Thanks for your patience with this!  Alexis Álvarez  MES Developer.  Koh Young America

docs.reply: Hello Robert,      Thanks for reaching out. I'm surprised to hear this, as we haven’t received any support ticket from Raytheon regarding CFX. Let me clarify how our CFX implementation works:     To fully comply with all the messages defined by the CFX standard, we use a tool called BRM. This tool can be installed on either the AOI or the Review station — but not on both. BRM connects to both the AOI GUI and the Review GUI to monitor events such as Read Barcode, Inspection Start, Inspection End, Review Start, Review End, etc. Based on these events, it generates and sends the appropriate CFX messages.  That said, at Koh Young, only one CFX instance is responsible for sending messages for both AOI and Review processes. As far as I know, the only critical data point that originates specifically from Review is the UnitInspected message. Depending on your configuration, you should receive one message after AOI completes the inspection, and—if the result is NG—you may receive a second message after the Review process. The main difference between these messages is the ActorType attribute, which helps identify whether it came from AOI(Robot) or Review(Human).       In order to make sure that the review data is being sent to the CFX server the only thing customer should be looking for is to have both sockets on GREEN state if one of them is GRAY this means that no connection is stablished.       If you have any questions please let me know.     Best regards

docs.reply: Hello Robert,      Even though BRM is installed in AOI it should be sending Review data as long as REVIEW socket is connected (GREEN state). If they are not receiving Review Data please let me know to send them a message directly.       Best regards

docs.reply: Hola Jose,     Muchas gracias por la credenciales. Durante la sesión remota nos apoyaron para validar las señales de smema en las que confirmamos que están saliendo las tablillas. Las señales SMEMA son las siguientes:   GOOD : 3-4 para indicar que está la tablilla lista para salir de la máquina  NG:  3-4 para indicar que está la tablilla lista para salir de la máquina. 7-8 para indicar que es NG   Por lo que nos comentan el buffer está esperando un cambio en 5-6 para las tablillas GOOD. Esto no es parte del standard que emplea la máquina por lo es necesario contactar al proveedor del buffer para que se manejen las tablillas y sus resultados acuerdo a las señales SMEMA de la AOI.  ​   Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hello Alonso,     Thank you for the logs.  It appears there is a mismatch between the security configuration in the CFX Plugin and the server settings. Could you please confirm the following details to help us resolve the issue:    Port and Protocol    AMQP typically uses port 5672 (unencrypted), while AMQPS usually uses port 5671 (encrypted).    Currently, the configuration is set to use AMQPS over port 5672, which may be causing the TLS handshake failure. Please verify that the correct port (5671 for AMQPS) is being used.      Encryption and Certificates    The server is configured in BRM as an AMQPS endpoint, which requires TLS encryption.    If the server does not support AMQPS, the connection cannot be established successfully. Please confirm whether TLS is enabled and properly configured on the server.      Authentication    The server has a specific user and password set up, which is currently configured in the plugin.    Please confirm that these credentials are correct and correspond to the user intended for production messaging.      Best regards

docs.reply: Hello Brett,     Could you please create a new ticket for our Help Desk peers to answer 1, 2 and 4 questions? As there are rules that can be set in the AOI GUI for CAM and External Scanners. Including alarming the machine if there is no barcode read.       Regarding   #3 When is the panel moved to the AOI?  After FlexFlow (MES) says the panel bar code is good? Or after AOI says the bar code is good?    The panel is moved inside the AOI after AOI says the barcode is good. Then the Barcode is sent to FlexFlow and a response is obtained. If it is negative the inspection is stopped.   For the special scenario where no Barcode is received and there are no rules set in the GUI to stop the machine. The inspection is completed, results are uploaded to the FlexFlow server and we have seen it answering with (1101) Panel Serial is not in correct format which stops the machine from releasing the board.      Best regards

docs.reply: Hello Simon,     I've forwarded this email to our official Koh Young support address (kya_order@kohyoung.com). At this moment, there’s nothing else required on your end—just make sure to copy that address in your next emails.     I’m glad to hear that you’re interested in adopting the CFX interface. We truly believe this standard will become the go-to protocol across the SMT industry.     Let me elaborate a bit more on what the BRM software is. BRM is a free tool that allows us (the developers) to create custom solutions based on customer requirements. By default, after installation, it won’t have any modules enabled. There are a few standard solutions available at no cost, but custom developments or license-based features would require an additional purchase order.     For CFX, specifically, it requires a license per machine ($2,200 each). Please let me know how many licenses you would need at your facility so I can provide a more accurate quotation.     Best regards

docs.reply: ​Hello Simon,      If you are talking about the standards (Free of charge) that are available here is the information about them:    We currently have two standard solutions that we can provide free of charge, as long as this is your first development. I’m sharing some information about these solutions so that you can evaluate them and decide which one would be more appropriate for your infrastructure and requirements.     KYStandard 2.0 This solution generates inspection information using output files (XML or CSV). Please review the document "KSMART BRM (KY FileExport V2.0).pdf" for detailed information regarding the content and output format of these files.     KYStandard 3.0 Unlike the previous one, this development uses JSON messages to send information via Web Services to a server on your side. Please review the document "KSMART BRM (KY Standard V3.0).pdf" to find a list of all the messages included in this development, along with the content of each message.     If you have any questions or need clarification, feel free to reach out, and we can review them together.     Best regards

docs.reply: Hola Jorge,     Como resumen de nuestra llamada anterior, entiendo que buscan automatizar la recolección de datos de sus equipos, un proceso que actualmente realizan equipo por equipo usando SPC para luego consolidar la información en un solo archivo.  Esto difiere un poco de nuestra propuesta inicial. Para abordar su necesidad de automatización y consolidación, sugiero dos caminos:   Opción 1: KSMART Server   Adquirir un servidor KSMART les permitiría consolidar los datos de todos sus equipos en una sola plataforma. Esto reducirá significativamente su carga de trabajo, ya que la herramienta recopilará automáticamente los reportes de todos los equipos, eliminando la necesidad de repetir el proceso individualmente. Además, tendrán acceso a todas las funcionalidades adicionales que ofrece el servidor KSMART.    Opción 2: SQL Server   La segunda opción es montar un servidor SQL. Mediante stored procedures, cada uno de sus equipos alimentaría directamente este SQL con la información necesaria. De esta forma, cuando necesiten generar un reporte, solo tendrán que ejecutar una query en el SQL para recopilar la información consolidada de todas las líneas.  ​   Espero sus comentarios para saber qué camino les resulta más adecuado.       Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: ​Hello Greg,      I was suspecting you were using X-Link. Please note that X-Link adapters are only supported by AEGIS, so we do not provide support for these integrations. The module essentially pulls data directly from the machine’s SQL server, meaning that as long as the machine is up and running with SQL properly configured, no additional setup is required on the machine side.     If you encounter any issues or need assistance with X-Link adapters, we recommend contacting eSupport@aiscorp.com. The AEGIS support team will assist you promptly.     Alternatively, if you are interested in IPC-CFX, this protocol is fully supported by AEGIS FactoryLogix and also includes complete support from Koh Young.  Please let me know if you have any further questions.     If you are having problems when using the general button (PASS/NG) I recommend you use this setting. This fill force the operator to classify all defects before enabling the general button.  ReviewConfig:       We have two options     1)Check : PCB level Pass/NG button will be activated when all items in the list have been viewed by the user (Not verified as Pass/NG). This was a request from a customer where they only wanted to verify the NG components and skip verification of Good components to save time. Viewed items will be highlighted in light yellow as seen below      2) Wait all: PCB Pass/NG button will be activated according to the component verification result Board Pass will be enabled when all defects are verified as Pass Board NG will be enabled when all defect are NOT verified as Pass.      Other than this, all the data handling falls under AEGIS. WE are unaware how they organize the data in their dashboard. Please let me know if you have any questions.      ​    Best regards

docs.reply: Hola Jorge Luis,  ​​   Para el primer barcode que mencionas al revisar si vemos que existen 2 envios de datos a su sistema MES a diferentes horas que concuerdan con los registros que compartiste:    ​   Lo que pasó fue lo siguiente: a las 12:14:28 el equipo leyó el siguiente barcode:  BAR|0|1|P1845703-00-C:SC7R25171NBDF01 ​   De acuerdo con la lógica de su sistema Unity, lo que se hace es utilizar el barcode master como base para los siguientes paneles, de la siguiente forma:    BarcodeMaster: P1845703-00-C:SC7R25171NBDF01    BarcodePaneles: BarcodeMaster sin los últimos 2 dígitos, agregando 01, 02, 03, 04, etc., dependiendo del número de paneles.    Siguiendo esta lógica, y tomando en cuenta que sus tablillas tienen 3 paneles, se hicieron las siguientes consultas a su sistema:    P1845703-00-C:SC7R25171NBDF02    P1845703-00-C:SC7R25171NBDF03    P1845703-00-C:SC7R25171NBDF04    A las 13:00 del mismo día, el equipo leyó el mismo barcode pero por el otro lane:  BAR|1|1|P1845703-00-C:SC7R25171NBDF01  Como la lógica es la misma y es el mismo barcode base, nuevamente se enviaron los mismos 3 paneles a su sistema:    P1845703-00-C:SC7R25171NBDF02    P1845703-00-C:SC7R25171NBDF03    P1845703-00-C:SC7R25171NBDF04    Conclusión de este primer caso: Tienen registros duplicados porque la misma tablilla entró dos veces al equipo, primero por lane 0 y después por el otro lane.   Para el otro barcode P1845703-00-C:SC7R25171NC2903, en los registros de la máquina solo existe 1 evento en el que nuestro equipo envía datos de este barcode a su sistema MES.       Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hola Fatima,      Gracias por la conexion Anydesk. Se reviso el flujo de mensajes y todo se ve en orden. Solo como informacion en general su sistema funciona de la siguiente manera:  1- Se inspecciona la unidad  2- Se envían datos MES a sus sistema una vez la inspección se termina.   3- Su servidor MES responde si hubo algun error con el mensaje que les proporcionamos o si  todo esta en orden.      Esta es una captura de los logs en una de las pruebas que realizamos:       El 1 es el mensaje que enviamos nosotros y el 2 es la respuesta de su servidor MES. Esta en coreano pero si hacemos la traducción significa "Entered" por lo que indica que todo esta funcionando de manera correcta.     De acuerdo a lo que platicamos el equipo local de IT no tenia idea clara de lo que estaba ocurriendo por lo que de su lado se escalara con el personal de Corea. Una vez tengan mas datos con gusto podemos agregar/quitar información si es que hace falta. Quedamos al pendiente de sus comentarios.      Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hola Hello, Edgar.  Respecto al NG gris del buffer es muy probable que esta sea la causa raíz: Por cada slot necesitamos 2 registros:        FRONT_RACK_PCBID_#    FRONT_RACK_PCBSTATUS_      El primero es un identificador para nosotros poder saber a que inspección hacer referencia con esa tablilla y el segundo se usa para saber el estatus de la tablilla, en los slots 2, 7 y 9 únicamente tenemos status (Unknown machine NG) ya que no tenemos identificador. El PLC no está acomodando el valor en el registro correspondiente. hay que involucrar al proveedor del buffer para verificar estos datos.      Para confirmar que este sea el error. ¿Podrías compartirnos el issue report de la maquina que tuvo esta falla desde BRM?  ​   Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hello Chris,     Glad to hear the software is working as expected.     Regarding the new feature you described, we do have functionality that aligns with your request—specifically, the ability to tie the activation of specific features to the loading of programs. However, this capability requires additional development. Due to its complexity, it cannot be added free of charge. Please let me know if you would like to receive a formal quotation for this feature (USD $1,500).     To give you a brief overview of how this feature works: once implemented, the first time you load a program it will retain the previously used configuration. For example, if a feature was turned off, it will remain off after loading the job. If you later turn it on, the next time you load that same program it will remember and apply that configuration automatically, overwriting the previous state. This behavior will apply individually to all jobs.      Please let me know if you have any questions or if you would like us to proceed with the quotation.     Saludos cordiales

To help us verify the configuration, could you please confirm the IP address of the Review Station machine by following these steps:    Press Windows + R to open the Run window.    Type cmd and press Enter.    In the Command Prompt, type:  ipconfig      Press Enter, and look for the "IPv4 Address" under either:    "Ethernet adapter" if using a wired connection, or    "Wireless LAN adapter" if using Wi-Fi.      Could you please share the IP addresses of both the AOI machine and the Review Station with us? This will help us verify if they match the configuration.  Once we confirm that both machines are on the same network segment and properly configured, we’ll proceed to re-test the connection.  Let us know if you encounter any issues while checking the IP addresses. Best regards

docs.reply: Hello TJ,  ​   I'll review the issue reports you're sending us. However, for the disconnection issue with KY-MES, I believe the fastest way to move forward would be to schedule a remote working session. Please let me know your availability—I'm quite flexible this week, so feel free to choose a time that best fits your schedule.  Regarding the message sequence: CFX does not enforce any specific order. The only guideline we follow comes from the documentation for the “Work Started” message:     “Sent by a process endpoint when the work-cycle for a unit or group of units starts.”       The UnitValidation message is triggered when reading the barcode, and barcode reading is part of the inspection process. Therefore, we send the WorkStarted message before the UnitsInspected message.     Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hello Jorge,     First of all:  Are they operating with internal or external flipper?  What is the name of this customer?     Regarding your comments:   Will there be barcodes for the bottom side? If not, we could assign the same barcodes for bottom or add an identifier like _B at the end.  During the inspection, if the Top side result is NG, the GUI Defect Viewer will show and then the board will load in the Review. We can not stop this board from showing on the Review but we can stop the classification until the Bottom Side has been inspected. The Bottom side will be inspected, no matter the result of Top side.  After both sides have been inspected, how should the reject be handled? Do they require a file export, an update to the result in SPC, a specific message?      Best regards
docs.cust: Hello team, A possible new customer asked us if it is possible to do a customization on the AOI system to attend to a specific demand described in the steps below: - Customer wants to inspect the Top...

docs.reply: Hello Jorge,     Please let us know if our understanding is correct and our comments from the BRM interaction.      Boards will be inspected using the TOP Side Job. Good and NG boards will be loaded to a buffer. (Is this correct ?)  Boards will be manually unloaded and flipped.  Boards will be inspected using the BOT Side Job.   TOP Good and BOT Good boards will exit the machine and follow the line.  TOP NG and/or BOT NG Boards will result in a NG SMEMA signal (It is possible for us to develop a KBR that executes this validation and signal)   Using a Scanner the boards will loaded to the Review Software.   It is required to scan the Barcode once for TOP and then once for BOT as they are two independent inspections in the Review software.  ​The TOP classification is independent from the BOT classification. As the boards are already out of the machine, it is only possible to use the result of the board in the NG Buffer and not to combine the TOP and BOT classification results at the NG Buffer. docs.reply: Hello Jorge,     Could you please share the details of the customer’s requirements for BRM? If the NG buffer is removed, the Review can be configured to input barcodes for classification, first for side1, then for side2 as independent boards. In this setup, BRM does not need to give an NG decision based on the results of both sides.    - When AOI finishes the inspection from this second side, AOI needs to reject the boards that present errors from any side (reject what already failed only top, reject if fails only bot, reject if it fails on both sides).      Best regards

docs.reply: Hello Christopher,     Thank you for the screenshot. Here is an explanation on the conditions of these export options:     Machine Good - The Log is generated for the GOOD results from the machine inspection, no review is required.  Machine NG - Log is generated for the NG results from the machine inspection, before the board is reviewed.  Review Pass - Log is generated After review for Pass classification results.  Review NG - Log is generated After review for NG classification results.  ​   When the review station is enabled it is common for Machine Good, Review Pass and Review NG as these could be considered the final results of a board.   Please disable Machine NG and if the issue continues,  please share the BRM logs by following the instructions in the attached manual.     Best regards

docs.reply: Hola Ajenandro/Savlador,     Muchas gracias por las respuestas.     Les comparto el archivo [P2.6.2.4]Flex_Guadalajara_North_DPI_V1.4.4.kbr actualizado   https://kohyoung-my.sharepoint.com/:f:/p/ramiro_rubio/EsgHsUIauHpEvQ1Phz4g8noBivq34wws8YD1IZHMhX6sKg?e=FhKoTI  En el cual pueden encontrar la sección File Export con los siguientes campos   Use - Habilitar/des habilitar la generación de los archivos de salida xml.  Show Duplicate Defects -  Con este campo habilitado, los archivos se generan con todos los defectos. Al des habilitarlo, los archivos se generaran sin duplicados por area.  Si en la ubicación Q23 existen 3 registros de falla, 2 son de insuficiencia y 1 de burbujas, en el XML de salida se tendrían solamente 2 defectos: Q23 con insuficiencia Q23 con burbujas     Events - Utilizado para seleccionar con qué resultado se requiere que se genere el archivo xml. Para resultados finales en los proceso en los que se clasifican todas las inspecciones, nomalmente se usa Machine Good, Review Pass y Review NG ya que los Machine NG deben ser clasificados en la Review.  Machine Good - Se genera el XML para los resultados de inspecciones GOOD  Machine  NG - Se genera el XML para resultados de inspecciones NG  Review Pass - Se genera el XML para resultados de classificacion Pass  Review NG - Se genera el XML para resultados de classificación NG     Export Level - Panel (Array) genera un archivo XML por panel hijo. PCB (Board) genera un archivo XML por inspección.  Panel Index - Identificador que se agrega a los archivos XML en caso de que solo haya un serial padre y existan 2 o más hijos. En caso de que cada panel tenga su propio serial, este cambo debe ser None  Tester Name -  El valor de este campo configurable se usa para el valor de testerName en el XML.  Stage - El valor de este campo configurable se usa para el valor de stage en el XML.  ​​​​              Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hola Alejandro,     El archivo que termina con "_R" se trata del resultado de la Review mientras que el otro contiene el resultado de la máquina. Los eventos que generan estos archivos son configurables, les comparto los significados de las opciones.      Events - Utilizado para seleccionar con qué resultado se requiere que se genere el archivo xml. Para resultados finales en los proceso en los que se clasifican todas las inspecciones NG, nomalmente se usa Machine Good, Review Pass y Review NG ya que los Machine NG deben ser clasificados en la Review.  Machine Good - Se genera el XML para los resultados de inspecciones GOOD  Machine  NG - Se genera el XML para resultados de inspecciones NG  Review Pass - Se genera el XML para resultados de classificacion Pass  Review NG - Se genera el XML para resultados de classificación NG        Referente al archvio DPI_CustomerCodes.xml que se encuentra en el sharepoint:  Es necesario que los valores de los Custom defects en Review 3.0 coincidan con los definidos en BRM > Codes > DPI > Defect. El archivo DPI_CustomerCodes.xml está basado en las imágenes que nos compartieron. Dentro de BRM este archivo puede actualizarse reemplazando los valores EX1-EX10 por los nombres de nuevos defectos que se den de alta en la Review.    En caso de que no coincidan  Es necesario reeemplazar el archivo ubicado en C:\KohYoung\KSMART Business Rules Management\XML\DPI_CustomerCodes.xml con la versión disponible en el SharePoint.      Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hello Rudy,     Bellow are the answers to the questions:   Is this IDNO representing/ unique each program or each board?  IDNO represents the PCBID, it is an identifier that increases for each board.    can be repeatable per month or can be at the same months?  It should not be repeated. If a PCBID is found repeated it is possible that more than one SPI is inserting data to the same SQL. Is this your scenario?    How to distinguish if in 1 month have same IDNO?  ​If there is more than one SPI adding data to the same database, you could verify the JobFileName if the machines are running different jobs.         Best regards

docs.reply: Hello Ignacio,     Here is an explanation on the conditions for the Result options:    Machine Good - The Log is generated for the GOOD results from the machine inspection, no review is required.  Machine NG - Log is generated for the NG results from the machine inspection, before the board is reviewed.  Review Pass - Log is generated After review for Pass classification results.  Review NG - Log is generated After review for NG classification results.  ​   When the review station is enabled it is common for Machine Good, Review Pass and Review NG as these could be considered the final results of a board.   Please disable Machine NG and if the issue continues,  please let us know. Thank you.     Best regards

docs.reply: Hello, Landon.  Currently BRM and KY-MES application are running at the same time on your SPI. In order to do this a feature inside BRM must be turned on called "Message Forwarding", basically this feature allows the communication of both application to the SPI GUI. The change I'd like to do is upgrade both BRM and KBR version to have access to a more recent change in this specific feature and modify this value to have a "delay" in this communication.  Now is in '2000' ms          Basically the steps to apply this new change are the following:  1-download the new BRM installer and KBR file  2-Back up current KBR version  3-Install new BRM(2.6.2.4) version and apply new KBR (2.0)    ​  I am sharing the following link with the complete package of files required for proper installation: LINK:   https://kohyoung-my.sharepoint.com/:f:/p/alexis_alvarez/EgbpWdJlD8VOvAJWanSNHnMBKUeurwvbOqsThQS8w1gszA?email=landon.murrell%40na.denso.com&e=Is6871  Included:   BRM Installer (.exe)  KBR Configuration File (.kbr)  Installation and configuration manual (.pdf)   Please follow the instructions in the manual. If you encounter any issues, do not hesitate to contact us for support.  I look forward to your feedback.   That’s it. After the update, we would like to monitor the equipment to verify if the issue has been resolved.  Best regards,  ​  Alexis Álvarez  MES Developer.  Koh Young America

docs.reply: Hola Carlos,     El día de hoy con el soporte de Felipe Valerio en sitio aclaramos el flujo de las consultas.     La primer llamada se realiza a la dirección construida a partir de la URL configurable Address (Barcode)   http://production-api2.42-q.com/mes-api/p2447dc6a/units/TLPCBMB013  Esta dirección nos responde con información de la tablilla. Si en la respuesta unit_type es igual a 99, se realiza una consulta al Address (Pallet).  -Si se realizó la consulta a Address (Pallet), las consultas de interlocking se realizan con los barcodes obtenidos de la API.  -Si unit_type fue diferente de 99, las consultas de interlocking se realizan con los barcodes leídos por la cámara.       Se identificó el caso en el que los archivos .xml de salida se estaban generando antes de hacer las consultas de Interlock. Para asegurarnos de que no se generen los archivos hasta que se realicen todas las consultas, es necesario aplicar las siguientes configuraciones. Adjunto manual actualizado.       Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hola Carlos-alberto,     Muchas gracias por levantar el ticket. Déjame te pongo en contexto respecto a la implementación que se está realizando en SPI, ya que al parecer es más compleja de lo que parece.  El equipo de SPI (José Francisco Mejía) solicitó agregar un campo para que ellos puedan ingresar usuario y contraseña. Sin embargo, se pidió que estas credenciales sean utilizadas en una nueva validación con su sistema 42Q, es decir, una nueva API a la cual se hará una solicitud para validar que dicho usuario esté autorizado para correr ese producto en la línea.  Dentro de nuestra lógica lo que realmente importa es la llamada a las API de su sistema; el archivo XML es únicamente un respaldo de esta transacción, de manera que quede un archivo como evidencia.  Este requerimiento, sin embargo, aún no se ha entregado a José ya que todavía se están definiendo todos los requisitos y pasos a seguir. Debido a todo lo que involucra este cambio, tendrá un costo de desarrollo para el equipo de SPI, y en caso de que se eligiera implementarlo también en los equipos Neptune, implicaría un costo adicional de 3000 USD para que lo tomen en consideración      Adjunto un vistazo de cómo se vería dicha aplicación en el desarrollo de BRM, así como de todo lo que implica este desarrollo:    [GuideLine e30]Summary [Interlocking  SPI 1.4] en la sección No 1:   Nueva consulta https://production.42-q.com/custom-api/p2448dc5/emp-validation/by-process?empno=Employee Number&password=Employee Password&processname=Process_name  Configurar que únicamente un usuario con privilegios de administrador en la GUI pueda habilitar o deshabilitar esta verificación, manteniéndola oculta para los demás usuarios.  Nuevos campos de Employee Number, Employee Password en Customer Property.  Process_name se requiere como un campo nuevo o debemos considerar los campos actuales de ProcessName(Top) y ProcessName(Bot)?  Se debe conservar los valores de Employee Number y Password indefinidamente? Se deben solicitar nuevos valores tras alguna acción o cambio en la GU​          ​    [GuideLine e30]Summary [Traceability SPI v1.4] en la sección No 1:   En el mensaje Json actualizar el campo employeeId para usar Employee Number.​   [GuideLine e30]Summary [Traceability SPI v1.4] en la sección No 2:   En el mensaje Json actualizar el campo employeeId para usar Employee Number y password con Employee Password.   Interlocking: Nuevo checkbox  para habilitar/deshabilitar la función solo visible para Administrador, manteniéndola oculta para los demás usuarios. ​ ​ Favor de revisar esta información y decidir si si se elije este camino como se hara en las SPI o si prefieren tomar el camino de crear los usuarios manualmente y ya de ahi nosotros recuperaríamos el dato.       Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hola, Luis:  Al ser una tabla corrupta, no parece posible recuperar los datos. En la máquina únicamente podíamos ver los logs después de las 19:00 horas.  Las tablas se crean por día, por lo que instalar una versión nueva de BRM no habría solucionado el problema. Realizar una instalación limpia es la manera ideal de resolver este issue, y por eso al cargar una imagen antigua del equipo se solucionó el error.    Si vuelve a presentarse un error similar, recomiendo seguir estos pasos:    Desinstalar BRM.    Ir a la ruta C:\KohYoung y renombrar la carpeta "KSMART Business Rules Management" como "KSMART Business Rules Management_OLD" (o cualquier otro nombre que permita identificarla como respaldo del BRM antiguo).    Una vez renombrada la carpeta, instalar nuevamente BRM.    Este es el procedimiento correcto para realizar una instalación limpia y que las tablas se vuelvan a crear correctamente.  Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hello, Luis.  El BadMark es una opción que permite identificar y marcar automáticamente aquellas PCBs dentro de un panel que se consideran defectuosas o no procesables.  Su propósito es:    Evitar que las máquinas posteriores intenten inspeccionar, ensamblar o soldar una PCB dañada.    Optimizar el flujo de producción, reduciendo tiempo y desperdicio.    Permitir que solo las PCBs “buenas” avancen en el proceso.    Alcance: normalmente se aplica a nivel de panel, donde cada sub-PCB puede ser marcada como Good o Bad.  En este caso, el sistema estaba marcando la PCB como Bad (omitida) debido a la información recibida en la lectura inicial del panel, lo cual puede deberse a:    Una marca física detectada en la PCB.    Una condición configurada en el sistema.    En resumen: la PCB fue omitida porque el sistema la clasificó como Bad de acuerdo con las reglas o condiciones activas en la máquina.  Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hello Evan,      I just forwarded this to our official support mail so we can have better tracking on our end. There’s nothing else you need to do except make sure that this address (kya_support@kohyoung.com) is included in your following responses.    We currently have two standard solutions that we can provide free of charge, as long as this is your first development. I’m sharing some details about these solutions so you can evaluate them and decide which one would be more appropriate for your infrastructure and requirements:      KYStandard 2.0 This solution generates inspection information using output files (XML or CSV). Please review the document "KSMART BRM (KY FileExport V2.0).pdf" for detailed information about the content and output format of these files.    KYStandard 3.0 Unlike the previous one, this solution uses JSON messages to send information via Web Services directly to your server (no files, only web service messages). Please review the document "KSMART BRM (KY Standard V3.0).pdf" for a full list of all included messages and their contents.    From your previous conversation, it seems you are looking to access the maximum amount of data possible. Please keep in mind that these two standards were not designed with that goal in mind. They provide useful information but not everything. Consider them as reference points—if needed, they can be scaled up later to meet your specific data requirements. Please check the information and let me know if you have any question.      Saludos cordiales
