docs.reply: Hello Evan,     There are a lot of request in this last mail, let me break them down so I can give you an answer to each one and also keep track of every request:  1️-Request about message format  “Our preference is to use a CFX.UnitsProcessed message… with a slightly modified measurement structure to accommodate additional attributes.”  Request:  Confirm whether using a modified CFX.UnitsProcessed message (with NumericMeasurement instead of InspectionMeasurementLean) is viable.  Response:  Just to clarify, I believe there might be a misunderstanding. Typically, inspection data is reported using the CFX.UnitsInspected message, not CFX.UnitsProcessed.  Please help me confirm this, as I think you may be referring to the UnitsInspected message — it includes the Measurements attribute, where you can use the NumericMeasurement class. The UnitsProcessed message, on the other hand, does not contain a Measurements field.  Regarding the new structure, I’m afraid the defined message structure is already embedded within the current software logic. While I can modify the fields inside the existing structure, I’m not able to change the structure type itself.  I’ll need to contact HQ to request that they include this new option. Once I receive a response from them, I’ll let you know their feedback.     2-Request to test standard CFX implementation first  Before beginning development on a custom CFX packet, would it be possible to trial your standard CFX implementation first to assess the impact on our cycle times?”  Request: Allow them to trial the standard CFX message on the machine before committing to custom development, to evaluate performance impact.  Response:  Yes if you decide to move forward we are able to provide a trial license up-to 120 days for you to test the software before making final decision.      3-Request for feedback on measurement fields  “We’ve also attached a list of the measurement fields we’re targeting... Could you advise whether any commonly used SPC fields are absent from our list?”  Request: Review their attached list of measurement fields and advise whether any common SPC fields are missing based on your experience with other customers.  Response:  I reviewed the Word document you shared and can see the specific measurements for each inspection type. However, I’m not entirely sure where you’re expecting to receive this information. In the first part of your message, you mentioned the NumericMeasurement class as the preferred structure to share the inspection values. However, this class only contains the following fields: (Value, ValueUnits, ExpectedValue, ExpectedValueUnits, MinimumAcceptableValue, MaximumAcceptableValue). Could you please clarify in which CFX message you would like to include all these fields(The ones inside the Word Document)? I’d like to make sure I fully understand this part before proceeding.     4-Request about message origin (AOI vs review station)  “Would it be feasible to write our custom message from the AOI machine and the standard message from the review station?”  Request: Confirm whether it’s technically feasible to send:    A custom message from the AOI machine, and    A standard message from the review station.     ​Response:  Can you please confirm if you mean by Custom message the one with NumericClass and the standard message the one using InspectionMeasurementLean?  If the answer is yes I'm afraid that it would only be possible to send 1 type of message it would be 1 or the other. However there is an option to select when you would like to receive this message  1-When AOI completed inspection  2-When Review completed Judgement   3-Both  ​    5-Request for stored procedure for inspection coverage reports  “We would find significant value in having a stored procedure write the inspection coverage reports… to an internal SQL database.”  Request: Provide or help create a stored procedure (or SQL view) that generates/writes the inspection coverage reports into their SQL database.  Response:  Yes, this can also be developed; however, it’s a completely separate feature from the CFX interface.  We would need you to create the SQL database and the stored procedure on your end, once this is taken care share with us the name of the store procedure and the parameters it accepts so that we can push the data after each inspection and send the data to it.     6-Request about image handling  “Should we attempt to transfer the inspection images to a network storage location as a part of this endeavor, or whether those would be best to manage as a separate [task]?”  Request: Provide guidance on whether inspection images should be:    Transferred to a network location as part of this project,    Handled as a separate process.     Response:    I believe it is possible to send the jpeg images in the CFX message, inside the Defects structure there is a field called DefectsImages that can be used to share this data please let me know if this is ok on your end.  (https://www.connectedfactoryexchange.com/html/P_CFX_Structures_Defect_DefectImages.htm)        Saludos cordiales
docs.cust: Hello Luis, First of all, please accept my apologies for the delayed response and thank you for your response and your willingness to work with us on our data needs. Before jumping into the responses...

docs.reply: Hi Serena:  ​        1️-Request about message format    KohYoung Response:   Just to clarify, I believe there might be a misunderstanding. Typically, inspection data is reported using the CFX.UnitsInspected message, not CFX.UnitsProcessed.   Please help me confirm this, as I think you may be referring to the UnitsInspected message — it includes the Measurements attribute, where you can use the NumericMeasurement class. The UnitsProcessed message, on the other hand, does not contain a Measurements field.   Regarding the new structure, I’m afraid the defined message structure is already embedded within the current software logic. While I can modify the fields inside the existing structure, I’m not able to change the structure type itself.   I’ll need to contact HQ to request that they include this new option. Once I receive a response from them, I’ll let you know their feedback.      SEL Response: Thank you for the clarification — you're absolutely right, I meant UnitsInspected.  Would it be simpler to send the custom inspection result blob to a REST API that we configure on our end? This could serve as a more flexible alternative to customizing the CFX structure, while still satisfying the objectives outlined in Section #3.   ​   Koh Young Response:   From my point of view, and given all the data you’re looking to obtain, YES, REST API calls would serve this purpose much better, as they can be fully customized based on your needs.  Usually, when a customer decides to go down this path, some documentation is provided — for example, what the API calls look like, the parameters they include, the data structure (XML/JSON) examples, and the expected response samples.       2-Request to test standard CFX implementation first   KohYoung Response:   Yes if you decide to move forward we are able to provide a trial license up-to 120 days for you to test the software before making final decision.       SEL Response: What steps are required to enable the trial license? We have a new line that we’d like to test on, which should be production-ready in approximately 1–2 weeks.    Additionally, if we observe that write operations are negatively impacting cycle times during the trial, would it be possible to disable data forwarding to mitigate those effects? Can this be done independently on our end, or would KohYoung need to be involved in that process?   ​   Koh Young Response:  Just let me know when you’d like to start the trial, as we need to generate a trial license that begins running as soon as it’s created (30/60/90/120 days).  Once the license is provided and loaded in BRM, CFX will be enabled on your machines. If the server that will receive our messages is already up and running, you’ll just need to configure the broker address and exchange to start receiving them.  Regarding the message forwarding, could you please elaborate a bit more on what that means in this context? I’d like to make sure I fully understand.      3-Request for feedback on measurement fields   KohYoung Response:   I reviewed the Word document you shared and can see the specific measurements for each inspection type. However, I’m not entirely sure where you’re expecting to receive this information.  In the first part of your message, you mentioned the NumericMeasurement class as the preferred structure to share the inspection values. However, this class only contains the following fields: (Value, ValueUnits, ExpectedValue, ExpectedValueUnits, MinimumAcceptableValue, MaximumAcceptableValue).  Could you please clarify in which CFX message you would like to include all these fields(The ones inside the Word Document)? I’d like to make sure I fully understand this part before proceeding.       SEL Response: I believe the alternative outlined in Section #1 may offer a simpler path forward. However, if that approach isn’t feasible, I’d be happy to elaborate on how I envisioned using the NumericMeasurement structure to achieve the same outcome, or we can discuss other alternatives.   ​   Koh Young Response:  ​Yes agree that REST API would be better if you want to get this type of data.       4-Request about message origin (AOI vs review station)   KohYoung Response:   Can you please confirm if you mean by Custom message the one with NumericClass and the standard message the one using InspectionMeasurementLean?    If the answer is yes I'm afraid that it would only be possible to send 1 type of message it would be 1 or the other. However there is an option to select when you would like to receive this message   1-When AOI completed inspection   2-When Review completed Judgement    3-Both       SEL Response: Thank you for the clarification – that answers my question.  You are correct, I did mean the one with the NumericClass and the standard using InspectionMeasurementLean.   Could you clarify how the data differs between the two locations, aside from the review station including the call outcome?   ​   Koh Young Response:  There are three main differences between the UnitsInspected message coming from the AOI and the one coming from the Review Station:    The UnitsInspected message from the AOI contains only the inspection result, whereas the one from the Review Station also includes the operator’s judgment.    There is a field inside the message called ActorType, which indicates the source of the result — AOI uses "Machine" while Review uses "Human".    Another field, Verification, differs as well — AOI uses "NotVerifiedYet", whereas Review may contain values such as "DefectConfirmed" or "DefectRejected"        5-Request for stored procedure for inspection coverage reports   KohYoung Response:   Yes, this can also be developed; however, it’s a completely separate feature from the CFX interface.   We would need you to create the SQL database and the stored procedure on your end, once this is taken care share with us the name of the store procedure and the parameters it accepts so that we can push the data after each inspection and send the data to it.      SEL Response: Thank you — we recognize this is a separate request, but I wanted to avoid layering on additional asks later and prefer to be transparent from the start.   If the REST API described in Section #1 is a viable path forward, could we proceed similarly in this case?   One additional question: could you help me understand the rationale behind pushing coverage reports after each inspection? Is there a way to make these pushes more event-driven — for example, only sending the job coverage report when a new job is created or when there’s an “Update Inspection Condition” status?  I am referring to the data exported from KSmart using the “download” button highlighted below.  ​   Koh Young Response:  Yes, the same applies to this bullet if we decide to move forward with the REST API solution.  Regarding the coverage report, I was under the impression that you were referring to another coverage report within the AOI. Thanks for clarifying that you were actually talking about the coverage report inside the KSMART Server.  This KSMART coverage report cannot be automated — it’s a manual process that needs to be performed by someone. I’m not able to create logic for this process, and as you mentioned, it only makes sense to perform it when there’s a change in the inspection conditions, not after every inspection.                  6-Request about image handling   KohYoung Response:   I believe it is possible to send the jpeg images in the CFX message, inside the Defects structure there is a field called DefectsImages that can be used to share this data please let me know if this is ok on your end.  (https://www.connectedfactoryexchange.com/html/P_CFX_Structures_Defect_DefectImages.htm)        SEL Response:  We’ll need to do some work on our end to determine where these images will be pushed, but in the meantime, we had a few preliminary questions:    Are we able to access “good” images in addition to those used for calls? If so, could you provide a rough estimate of how many images are typically collected during an inspection?  We understand this varies by job, but any general guidance on what’s collected would be helpful.     Given that CFX only transmits images related to defects, we’re assuming a different approach would be needed to access the full image set. Could you confirm if that’s correct?    Koh Young Response:  Yes, good images can be generated; however, please keep in mind that this process is very demanding in terms of storage, as by default only defect images are stored. If you’d like to include good images, you would need to enable this option — but be aware that storage space may run out fairly quickly.  Also consider that the normal operation cycle time is measured without storing good images. Enabling this option will certainly increase the cycle time, since more data needs to be processed and saved.  You are correct — CFX only considers defect data in its structure. Sharing any good inspection data through CFX would require modifications to the standard.           Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hola Gabriel,     En base a las imágenes que compartes, el estado que se observa en el buffer (slot en gris con texto “NG”) está asociado a una falla de comunicación con el buffer.  Déjame explicar un poco más: cuando una tablilla sale del equipo AOI, este envía dos datos al buffer:    Identificador → para saber a qué tablilla se hace referencia.    Status → para indicar si está pendiente de clasificar o si fue GOOD.        El buffer recibe estos dos valores y los acomoda en los slots disponibles. Por cada slot hay dos registros: uno para el identificador y otro para el status de la tablilla.  Cuando aparece un slot en gris significa que el buffer solo acomodó uno de los valores: el status. El valor que falta es el identificador. Por esta razón, cuando das click en el slot no se carga ninguna falla: el PLC del buffer no tiene el identificador colocado y, por lo tanto, no sabemos qué inspección mostrar.    Esta conclusión se basa únicamente en las imágenes compartidas. Para confirmar que este sea el caso, sería necesario que nos permitan conectarnos al equipo o, en su defecto, que nos compartan el issue report (adjunto manual con los pasos para recuperarlo).     Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hello Everyone,     Based on the comments from the Hayawin team, it appears they are attributing the root cause to the KY machine not sending the expected ID information. However, this does not align with our analysis.  Our findings indicate that the issue lies with the buffer not responding with the expected acknowledgment message, as defined in the sequence.     In order to clear this doubt we enabled all the logs on our machine and tested a board and successfully identified the message sent by the KY machine to the buffer. This message is a write operation to memory addresses 10000–10099, as specified by the protocol, with the ID as the payload.  However, the PLC is not responding with the expected acknowledgment, causing the sequence to time out.     With this new information we are requesting for your comments and if there are any other message from the KY machines you need to look at, we can help you.      Message=<01#     Best regards
docs.cust: Saludos Gabriel Alejandro Medina

En este equipo tenemos configurado que “case” el modulo con el barcode para que le de flujo Harman con la versión 2.7.5.0 no tenemos ningún problema pero al realizar el upgrade a la versión 2.8.1.3 o la versión 2.8.0.0 del GUI nos aparece el siguiente mensaje “Include Special Characters. Check Special Characters Option”  Es necesario realizar un cambio de configuración. Es posible tener una conexión remota a la máquina    Revisando tambien el BRM, tambien nos aparece el siguiente mensaje  Los errores que aparecen en BRM se deben a que no se encontraron reglas de BRM para el Job. Para crear estas reglas en caso de que las requieran para este programa, es necesario abrir el Job en la GUI y seguir los siguientes pasos:  Hacer click en BRM > Customer Property > Save   Agregar valores en las columnas Characters Info y Vendor name que se encuentran en BRM > Customer Data Grid > OCR Vendor y hacer click en Save           Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hello Shahar,      Thanks for your help it was really useful. Let me share a bit of context on the current situation. Koh Young developed a library that handles the ArchFX communication, and this library has been updated several times over the years. Only the latest version (1.5) includes the additional fields in the Measurement section as well as all the other enhancements.     A few weeks ago, we received a request from Israel Chepovesky (Flex) to upgrade all Koh Young America sites to the latest BRM version in order to migrate from HTTP to HTTPS. As part of that upgrade, I also plan to update the ArchFX library to  version 1.5. Once this migration is completed, all America sites will be sending the extended data fields you are looking for. We plan to have this ready by the end of the month. I can provide updates of the progress as we advance.      Please let me know if you have any questions about the information shared.          Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hello John,     1. When we fail a board on the machine it then shows up on the off-line review station. We then review it on the review station, after review is done it does not trigger the machine to release the board, It's now stuck in the machine. What can we do to resolve this?     Please confirm that the Review > Net Client > MES Option > Result to MESIF is enabled        2. when we start the review software on the review station we then get the following error on the machine. Closing the review software on the station stops the error message. Is there something we can do about this?    The software generating the warnings is BRM. This application communicates with both the AOI GUI and the Review software through network sockets. If multiple instances of the Review software are configured to use the same socket, communication conflicts will occur, resulting in the warnings you’re seeing.    It is technically possible to configure multiple Review software instances to connect to the same BRM using different sockets. However, when using Review Feedback, only one of these instances can trigger the board exit. Any additional Review software connected will not have the ability to initiate board exit from the machine.      Best regards

docs.reply: Hello Felix,     If I understood you correctly, you’re asking the following:     Is it possible to recover XML files from previously reviewed boards through KBRM? I know the XML file is generated when a board is reviewed either by the machine or by the operator at the review station. But can I go through the serial numbers on the review station and regenerate the XML file?  ​   In other words, you want to repeat the review process in order to generate the "ReviewCompleted" event for a board that has already been reviewed. This is indeed possible in the way you described.  By default, Review Main Mode is set to "Buffer Conv. + Call". In this mode, only pending boards are shown—you cannot go back to a previous inspection to re-classify. To enable this, you need to switch the mode to "Barcode (Handy Barcode)". In this mode, you can bring up the previous inspection results (if already reviewed) as many times as needed, and the system will regenerate the XML accordingly.     Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hello Shanghui,     Thanks for your time to take the call. I think it was very usefull as now we have a greater understanding of the application you are trying to build. Please let me share the notes I took and the PPT that we created.     1, Reported NG image (mandatory).  JPEG   Filename Struncture: CRD_DefectType.jpeg  2, Reference image or Golden Image (preferably same format and size as defect image)  USE a GOOD PCB as Reference/Golden Image  2.1 alternative image.  PENDING  3, Inspection bounding boxes (w-box) corresponding to component designators.  Since the Images are going to be of the Specific component only, no need to add w-box.  4, Text information of board serial number, component designator corresponding to the image and board inspection time.  Xml Extension with this structure:    Is it going to be a XML file per image?  YES  Sample directory structure C:\OutputFiles\JobName\Date\Barcode\C18_Missing\C18_Missing.jpeg & C18_Missing.xml & ProgramParameters.xml     Optional info:  AOI inspection program file (including coordination of inspection, Inspection bounding boxes (w-box)and parameters).  X & Y, length, width.  Parameters: Exposure time,  This data would be in a XML file as well…     Diagram:    1) After inspection is completed Data generated by AOI is written to the SQL  2) Once all data is in SQL, the BRM software will retrieve that inspection data to process it  3) BRM software will create the NG images as well as the XML files  4) Once files are created Apulis will take those files to use their AI model  5) Apulis will create a result based on the input images and this will only be relevant to Foxconn internally, no need to modify anything on the KY Software.      We will start to develop this solution and provide the sample files. Please let me know if you have any questions I can help you with.      Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hello Tristan,     ​If we look at AOI, a single component can report multiple defects (e.g., OCR/OCV, upside-down, coplanarity). In contrast, in SPI, when reviewing defects in the Defect View at the end of inspection, you will only see one defect per pad. Within the SPI configuration, you can define the priority of the defect types, and based on that priority, the system will display the corresponding defect.   SPI GUI>Settings>Inspection>InspectionOptions>DisplayOptions    ​    Is it ever possible for there to be more than one Measurement per inspection? Only 1 measurement per-defect  Is it ever possible for there to be more than one Defect per inspection? One defect per-inspection.       Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hello Tristan,     ​Thanks for todays connection, just as a quick recap we tackle down both issues you were seeing.   ​   1- The first one was related to this folder C:\KohYoung\KY-3030 being in read-only mode and thus not allowing BRM to access some files creating the issue where both applications were not communicating properly.      2- We tried to reproduce the issue however we realized that it was not possible to reproduce the issue using Single Panel PCB. After using a PCB with multiple Panels it was really easy to reproduce the issue, just using the big PASS button for the overall result. By doing this the issue occurred in which overall result is fine but individual defects are marked as defect confirm, however if you classify all defects and only then use the big PASS button there is no issue in the CFX message. In order to make sure the operator always classify each defect individually you could potentially use this option inside KY-Config "Verifying all defects" this will force the process and you will always have correct values in your message.            This option would be discussed on your end so please let us know any update to see if we can help you in any other way.      Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hello Tristan,  ​   Reset Barcode: If this option is disabled and an inspection is interrupted (while still inspecting the same board), the system will not read the barcode again since it recognizes it as the same PCB. However, for BRM, we require this trigger to occur — therefore, we instruct the GUI to reset the barcode so that it is read every time.  Use Master Barcode for Multipanel: I’m not entirely sure about this one — I just found that it works, lol. From what I understand, it applies to special cases where multiple panels are part of a single PCB but with only one barcode. If this option is disabled, the system likely expects a separate barcode for each panel.     Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hola, Carlos.  Te comparto los pasos para compartir la carpeta de la AOI con la review station y generar los archivos en esta misma ruta:    En la AOI vamos al disco D: y damos click en propiedades          Seleccionamos Everyone y damos click en add  ​  Nos aseguramos de que la carpeta tenga permisos de Read/Write antes de dar clic en Share.   ​   ​  Copiamos la ruta de la carpeta compartida.    Ejemplo: \\DESKTOP-VHROLJH\Output      ​ ​ ​ ​  Abrimos el software BRM en la review station y pegamos la ruta compartida en el Export path. Una vez pegado damos en guardar.      ​    También es recomendable abrir esta carpeta desde el explorador de archivos en la review para verificar que podamos visualizar sus contenidos.   Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hello Eric,     The way our software works is as follows:    AOI inspects the PCB.    AOI stores the inspection data in SQL.    BRM retrieves the data from SQL.    BRM transforms that data into the format the customer requires.    To make this work, BRM needs to know where the SQL database is located. This includes the IP address, database name, user, and password. Since this information is already stored in the AOI configuration, when an AOI machine is connected to BRM we normally run a script that reads the AOI configuration file and retrieves those fields for BRM.  In this case, the script appeared to be reading old data. The SQL IP being used did not match the one configured on the AOI. When I checked the KohYoung directory, I noticed multiple backup .ini files, so my assumption is that the script was pulling the information from an outdated file. Because of this mismatch, BRM could not access the SQL database after inspection, and that’s why the error occurred.  To resolve the issue quickly, I manually entered the SQL credentials instead of relying on the script. This allowed production to continue, although the true root cause still needs to be investigated further (I didn’t have time to dig deeper at that moment).  How have the lines been running since my last connection? Have you noticed any further issues?     Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hello, Sawyer.  I don’t recommend using OCR to retrieve data from each panel, since OCR inspection is not 100% reliable. OCR can sometimes misinterpret characters (for example, confusing “O” with “0” or “I” with “1”), which makes it less consistent for this type of application.  The best approach would be to print a QR code, barcode, or DataMatrix on each panel, ensuring a 100% accurate read.  Best regards / Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hello, Sawyer.  This request can only be achieved through custom development in BRM. Since this type of inspection is not 100% reliable, I’m not able to proceed with the request. We can develop a solution to read these numbers, but these “barcodes” CANNOT be assigned as a “PANEL Barcode.” They can only be added as an “OCR inspection” in the export file. The GUI does not provide OCR barcode as an option.  Best regards / Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hello Nathan,     The SPI and AOI machines do have standard outputs that can be in csv or xml format. The files after each panel inspected can be outputted to local or network drive.   We currently have two standard solutions that we can provide free of charge, as long as this is your first development. I’m sharing some details about these solutions so you can evaluate them and decide which one would be more appropriate for your infrastructure and requirements:      KYStandard 2.0 This solution generates inspection information using output files (XML or CSV). Please review the document "KSMART BRM (KY FileExport V2.0).pdf" for detailed information about the content and output format of these files.    KYStandard 3.0 Unlike the previous one, this solution uses JSON messages to send information via Web Services directly to your server (no files, only web service messages). Please review the document "KSMART BRM (KY Standard V3.0).pdf" for a full list of all included messages and their contents.        Best regards

docs.reply: Hola, Luis.  Ya estuve revisando el desarrollo y me di cuenta que actualmente ya lo manejamos de esta manera de nuestro lado; el problema es que recibimos como respuesta del servidor un valor completamente NULL o vacío. Esto provoca que en nuestro código se ejecute el bloque catch, arrojando la excepción que se me reportaste.     Hoy en día, su desarrollo ya cuenta con la validación para ignorar los atributos con valor null y enfocarse únicamente en el atributo status (ACK). Sin embargo, cuando el JSON llega completamente vacío, no es posible parsearlo.  Adjunto imagen de referencia de nuestro código.      Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hola, Team. Buen día.  De acuerdo con la documentación inicial, está definido que obtendremos una respuesta del servidor con el siguiente formato JSON:      ​  Podemos realizar una modificación para evitar parsear el mensaje que recibimos en caso de que el servidor no envíe un formato JSON válido.  La idea sería únicamente buscar las palabras “ACK” o “NACK” dentro del string de respuesta, sin importar si el contenido es JSON o no.  Mi duda es: ¿desean que, en caso de que el string de respuesta sea null o esté vacío (""), lo manejemos como si fuera un ACK para que la maquina no se detenga?  En caso de que deseen proceder con estos cambios el precio del desarrollo sería de $2500 dlls Quedo atento a sus comentarios.    Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hola Hector,     Entiendo completamente lo que dicen.. sin embargo creo que hay una confusion el escenario que vimos no es el que describen      Lo que ustedes creen que llega -> Una estructura JSON con algunos campos en NULL    Lo que en realidad nos llega -> Ni siquiera llega una estructura JSON vacía.... lo que recibimos es una respuesta vacía NULL completo   NULL     Es aquí la confusion, si nosotros aplicamos la lógica que describes:     La gran mayoría de las veces el JSON llegara sin valores nulos Fig. A por lo tanto se puede continuar con el mismo proceso, pero al genera la excepción tendría que buscar el campo/palabra clave “status”:”ACK” en la información recibida Fig. B (morado).  Si al encontrar el campo “status”:”ACK”, proceder normalmente.  Si al encontrar el campo “status”:”NACK”, proceder a mostrar un error en pantalla  ​   De igual manera arrojaría la excepción ya que estamos buscando la palabra ACK en un objeto nulo, si gustan podemos aplicar la lógica que describen, solo contemplen que esto no solucionara el escenario que se detecto. Así mismo creo que si aun quedan dudas la mejor manera de aclararlas y avanzar para una solución es tener una llamada por teams, favor de indicarme si desean que busquemos un espacio.          Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hola Hector,     El timeout está definido en esta sección con un valor de 30 segundos; sin embargo, este es el tiempo máximo que esperamos por una respuesta de su servidor.     BRM>CustomerProperties>FlexFlow>Timeout (ms) = 30000        En los logs que compartes:      La primera línea corresponde al registro de que nuestro equipo (KY) envió el mensaje a su sistema.     En la segunda línea registramos que recibimos la respuesta de su servidor, y en efecto, esta llegó muy rápido por eso ocurre en el tiempo que mencionas (495 ms). No es que tengamos un timeout muy pequeño, sino que el servidor respondió de manera casi inmediata.    La tercera línea muestra el error al intentar buscar valores en un objeto vacío (NULL).         Con respecto a lo que mencionas (“Todo indica que es la opción 2”), ¿podrías por favor elaborar un poco más al respecto? El equipo de Koh Young ha utilizado logs del equipo con timestamps para respaldar los hallazgos. ¿De casualidad ustedes cuentan con logs del servidor para corroborar que la respuesta fue enviada en este caso en particular? Si es así, ¿podrían compartirlos por este medio, por favor?  Asimismo, para despejar cualquier duda, si este error ocurre de manera constante, mi recomendación sería instalar en el equipo Koh Young un analizador de paquetes (Wireshark). Este programa registra todo el tráfico de red, mostrando tanto los paquetes que salen del equipo como los que entran desde el exterior. Con esta herramienta podríamos confirmar con certeza qué información está llegando desde su servidor, en caso de que el log de BRM no sea suficiente.  Quedamos al pendiente de sus comentarios.      Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hello Zach,     There are two ways I know to connect our machines to their servers:       CFX: Using the CFX standard is one way to do this. This standard was created by IPC and defines a wide set of messages for all potential machine events. Using this method will require enabling each machine with a license.    XLink Adapter: This other method was developed by AEGIS and basically harvests our machine data directly from our SQL server. This method works well as no license from Koh Young is needed, and we don't have to apply any additional logic for it to function. As long as the machine is operational, the XLink Adapter should be able to retrieve and use the data. However, the only downside is that we provide very limited support since we are not required to do anything other than ensure a functional machine for this to work. All support for this method should be provided by Aegis. If you want to have full support from Koh Young, please consider using the first method.        If you requiere a custom file export, please share with us the details of the file as: the extension, the contents, its structure, naming conventions and if possible an example so we can develop this requirement . Thank you.    Best regards

docs.reply: Hello Jonas,     Sorry for my late reply, let me break down what happens when a PCB is inspected and placed in the buffer:     1- PCB get inspected by AOI  2- When Inspection is completed a communication sequence is initiated:  in which koh young machine activates some registers in the PLC and also it sends the PCBGUID (identifier) of the PCB that is about to enter the buffer   4- Once this sequence is completed in the PLC memory map of the Buffer we should see 2 registers associated with each slot. 1 for Status and 1 for identifier the error that you are seeing: This means that we attempted to update the result based on the review process, but when we searched for the specific identifier, it could not be found in the PLC memory map.  There are two possible scenarios:    Early sequence execution – The Buffer PLC may be running a sequence earlier than expected, deleting the register associated with the PCB before we attempt the update.    Heavy workload condition – Since this only occurs sporadically, it’s likely happening under high production load. In such cases, the buffer may become stressed, and the PLC might not behave as expected.    Recommendation: Lower the communication bit rate between the buffer and the Koh Young machine. The default speed is 115200, but please reduce it to 57600. This adjustment must be applied on both sides (buffer and Koh Young) for proper synchronization. On the Koh Young side, you only need to update the setting here:         Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hello Gerardo,     There are two ways I know to connect our machines to their servers:       CFX: Using the CFX standard is one way to do this. This standard was created by IPC and defines a wide set of messages for all potential machine events. Using this method will require enabling each machine with a license.    XLink Adapter: This other method was developed by AEGIS and basically harvests our machine data directly from our SQL server. This method works well as no license from Koh Young is needed, and we don't have to apply any additional logic for it to function. As long as the machine is operational, the XLink Adapter should be able to retrieve and use the data. However, the only downside is that we provide very limited support since we are not required to do anything other than ensure a functional machine for this to work. All support for this method should be provided by Aegis. If you want to have full support from Koh Young, please consider using the first method.        If you require a custom file export, please share with us the details of the file as: the extension, the contents, its structure, naming conventions and if possible an example so we can develop this requirement . Thank you.  ​       Ramiro Rubio
docs.cust: Hello KY team, good afternoon, We are Review the Result data from SPI and AOI to Traceability matters and to try to transfer it to FactoryLogix. But we wanted to ask: * Where is all the result data...

docs.reply: Hello Gerardo,     Apologies for my incomplete response.  1.- Where is all the result data located for SPI and AOI?  The AOI saves the inspection results to a SQL server, the address of this sever can be identified and configured using the AOIConfig tool.    The inspection results for SPI are stored in MS Access files, the root path for these files can identified and configured using the KYConfig tool.     ​   2.- Is there a text file or something that contains the Pass/Fail results per Serial number? For SPI and AOI  3.- Is there a text file or something that contains the detail of results/components flagged, Pass/Fail per component and per Serial number? For SPI and AOI  By default, the machines do not produce text files with results by serial number or by component for each inspection      If you require such information,  we are able to customize the output of the machines using KSMART Business Rules Management (BRM).  We currently have two standard solutions that we can provide free of charge, as long as this is your first development. I’m sharing some details about these solutions so you can evaluate them and decide which one would be more appropriate for your infrastructure and requirements:   KYStandard 2.0 This solution generates inspection information using output files (XML or CSV). Please review the document "KSMART BRM (KY FileExport V2.0).pdf" for detailed information about the content and output format of these files.  KYStandard 3.0 Unlike the previous one, this solution uses JSON messages to send information via Web Services directly to your server (no files, only web service messages). Please review the document "KSMART BRM (KY Standard V3.0).pdf" for a full list of all included messages and their contents.     Best regards
docs.cust: Hola Ramiro, good afternoon, Thank you for the feedback! Yes, our engineering team is already working with them in the implementation of xLink. But, I wanted to review with you for folder locations...

docs.reply: Hello Gerardo,     AOI result data: How do I know on which folder is located? Shouldn’t this mention a path?   This information is stored in a SQL database rather than as files in a traditional folder structure. It should only be accessed by the Machine, Review, and SPC software for reading and writing results.  Regrettably, due to company policies we do not allow third-party systems from accessing our SQL servers due to concerns about data integrity and performance. Nonetheless, we provide various alternative methods to access this information through standard interfaces such as IPC-CFX (recommended), SECS/GEM, iTAC, Panasonic iLNB, ASYS Pulse, and more. We also offer custom options including Web Services and Output Files.     SPI result data:  But how to read it? Get conclusion/results out of them?   This information should only be accessed by the SPC Plus software.     KYStandard2.0:  ​I am guessing the highlighted path, is where all the CSV will be stored? Which is local. Could that path be configured? Or if it is local, would it represent a limitation for the storage space? The highlighted path is a configurable field where files will be stored. If the machine has access to a desired network folder, this path can be set accordingly.      KYStandard3.0:  Where are the JSON messages stored? his implementation requires your IT team to set up the web service infrastructure needed to receive and process the JSON messages published by the machine. These messages are not stored on the machine.    Both options seem really good (specially 2.0), can they both be installed/working at the same time? Both features can be integrated to work at the same time. The files and the messages would contain the same information and results.     Best regards

docs.reply: Hello John,     Thanks for the clarification. I’m somewhat familiar with SECS/GEM I do know it’s a widely used standard in the industry. Within the standard, a set of Stream Functions (SFs) are defined.  In our past implementations with other customers, they’ve typically provided detailed documentation describing how each message is expected to be received, since every SF can have a different structure. A list of SFs is usually included, indicating which ones are required and which are optional. For example, one customer provided a 158 page document describing every SF they wanted implemented, along with examples of each.     In addition to the SF list, it’s also very helpful to include information about alarms (S5F1/F2), events and report mappings (S6F11/F12, S2F33/S2F35), variables or VIDs (S1F3/F4), and the communication parameters such as IP, port, and connection mode. These details allow us to fully understand the scope of the SECS/GEM interface and ensure everything aligns with your expectations as this is specific for every customer.     Here are a few screenshots of what we’re looking for:     Sample Images from other client documentation:         Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hola, Rosillo:  En caso de ser necesario, el valor de typeNo se encuentra en el cuerpo del XML, dentro de la etiqueta <resHead>. en la nueva actualización    Con respecto a la actualización de KY-MES, al tratarse de un software legacy de Koh Young, no podemos brindar soporte para versiones anteriores. Mi recomendación es asegurarse de desinstalar correctamente la versión más reciente e instalar nuevamente la versión anterior o validar con otra maquina que tengan corriendo la misma versión de KY-MES en caso de que sea necesario.  Cabe mencionar que la versión nueva instalada de KY-MES es una actualización del desarrollo y los cambios observados son resultado de requerimientos previamente solicitados hace algunos años. Por lo tanto, el comportamiento que se está presentando no corresponde a un error, sino a la implementación de mejoras solicitadas previamente.    La última versión de KY-MES disponible actualmente es KY_MES_SPI_Setup_x86_4.3.0.7579, la cual incluye todas las funcionalidades solicitadas a lo largo de los años.    En caso de requerir alguna modificación personalizada, sería necesario realizar una migración de KY-MES a KSMART BRM, nuestro software actual para la integración de MES. Cabe destacar que esta migración puede ser un proceso complejo y tedioso, ya que requiere planificar pruebas detalladas para validar que todas las funcionalidades y datos se transfieran correctamente, evitando cualquier impacto en la operación de la línea de producción. Una vez realizada esta migración podemos agregar o implementar nuevos cambios en el desarrollo.    Quedo a disposición para cualquier duda o aclaración adicional.  Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hola, Mario.  Hemos identificado que este comportamiento corresponde a un bug en la versión actual de BRM. Como medida temporal, podemos configurar los customer codes, guardar el archivo XML y aplicarlo cada vez que se cierre y vuelva a abrir BRM. Cabe mencionar que si únicamente se cierra la ventana con la X, la configuración no se ve afectada; el problema ocurre solamente cuando se reinicia el equipo o se termina el proceso manualmente.  Adicionalmente, levantaré un ticket directamente con el equipo en Corea para dar seguimiento y asegurar una solución definitiva a este tema.  Una vez configurado los customer codes podemos dar doble click en la siguiente opción:   Copiamos y guardamos el XML configurado     Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hola, Sandra.  Con respecto a tu solicitud, confirmo que esta implementación es posible. El costo del desarrollo es de 2000 USD.  La solución contempla los siguientes puntos:    Configuración de contraseña: Se agregará la opción de establecer un password definido desde customer property, lo que permitirá mayor flexibilidad y control interno por parte de su equipo.    Cierre automático por inactividad: Se podrá configurar un tiempo determinado en minutos, de modo que si no se detecta actividad dentro de ese intervalo, el sistema BRM cerrará las ventanas activas y realizará el cierre de sesión de manera automática.    Con estas funciones se busca reforzar la seguridad en el acceso al software, Quedo atento a tus comentarios para validar si procedemos con esta implementación y en caso de ser así hacerles llegar la cotización formal.    Saludos!!    Alexis Álvarez  MES Developer.  Koh Young America

docs.reply: Hola Fred,     Gracias por la conexion, pudimos ver el error en vivo y adicional revisar los logs de BRM para ver la comunicacion con BRM y el PLC del buffer.   Se descubrio que el PLC no esta respondiendo al ultimo ACK de la secuencia, cada evento (PCB NG Insert, PCB GOOD Insert, Review NG, Review Pass) tienen un intercambio de mensajes para poder lograr el escenario correctamente (archivo tecnico adjunto) es necesario que los proveedores del Buffer revisen la logica del PLC para averiguar porque el PLC no envia el ultimo paso de la secuencia.           De momento nos pasamos a desconectar debido a que esta es una implementacion nueva, el horario de after hours esta dedicado para equipos ya instalados y que se encuentren caídos. El día de mañana podemos ayudarlos sin ningún problema (8:00am - 5:00pm)       Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hello, Vasilli. ​  We currently have two standard solutions that we can provide free of charge, as long as this is your first development. I’m sharing some information about these solutions so that you can evaluate them and decide which one would be more appropriate for your infrastructure and requirements.     KYStandard 2.0: This solution generates inspection information using output files (XML or CSV). Please review the document "KSMART BRM (KY FileExport V2.0).pdf" for detailed information regarding the content and output format of these files.  KYStandard 3.0: Unlike the previous one, this development uses JSON messages to send information via Web Services to a server on your side (No files, only web service messages). Please review the document "KSMART BRM (KY Standard V3.0).pdf" to find a list of all the messages included in this development, along with the content of each message.     I’ll be awaiting your feedback once you have decided which solution works best for you. Once I receive that information, I will proceed to create and share the necessary files so you can complete the installation on your machines. If you have any questions or need clarification, feel free to reach out, and we can review them together.    Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hello Matt,     We've updated the KBR to address the issues you reported with the yield alarm. Here's a summary of the change:  What changed: When the operator acknowledges a Level 1 or Level 2 yield alarm by clicking OK, the red light and audible alarm now turn off immediately — even if the FPY is still below the threshold. Previously, the light and buzzer stayed on after the notification was dismissed.  This should also resolves the issue where disabling the audible alarm on Critical 2 was preventing the machine from sounding its normal inspection-fail alarm. The root cause was that BRM's tower lamp command was persisting after acknowledgment and overriding the machine's own alarm behavior. Now, once the alarm is acknowledged, the machine regains full control of its tower lamp and buzzer.​      https://kohyoung-my.sharepoint.com/:f:/p/ramiro_rubio/EgZdupt7E6JFkE-jAPmfBHkB-Ivt1hV4S9EQul2BFmpiDw?e=bABAXX     Best regards,     Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hello Matt,  Thank you for testing the update and for the video — glad to hear the tower lamp and message boxes are working as expected.  Regarding the yield numbers, the FPY alarm calculates the pass rate using the machine's production count (GOOD and NG) for the current run — it resets when the job changes. However, I'd like to take a closer look at the exact values being used in the calculation to determine why it's showing a different number than the machine's production stats.  Could you please share the BRM logs by following the instructions in the attached manual?  ​ https://kohyoung-my.sharepoint.com/:f:/p/ramiro_rubio/EgZdupt7E6JFkE-jAPmfBHkB-Ivt1hV4S9EQul2BFmpiDw?e=bABAXX               Saludos cordiales
docs.cust: Good Morning, I uploaded a folder to the shared file location titled "782026 FPY Update." Just wanted to show how the light tower was working as well as the message boxes, so I included a video of...

docs.reply: ​Hello Matt,  Thank you for the logs — I was able to identify the issue. The pass rate shown in the FPY alarm was lower than the machine's because reviewed boards (teaching and false calls) were being double-counted in the calculation. Each time a board went through teaching, it was counted once as NG and again as PASS, inflating the total board count and bringing the pass rate down.  For example, with your logs: the machine showed 121 boards produced at 76.9% pass rate, but BRM was calculating with 150 total boards (the extra 28 being the teaching boards counted twice), resulting in 62.7%.  I've corrected the formula so BRM now uses the same board count as the machine. Please download [P2.8.2.2]Gentex_Zeeland_AOI_V4.1.4.kbr from the SharePoint.  https://kohyoung-my.sharepoint.com/:f:/p/ramiro_rubio/EgZdupt7E6JFkE-jAPmfBHkB-Ivt1hV4S9EQul2BFmpiDw?e=bABAXX            Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hello, William.  Please note that X-Link adapters are only supported by AEGIS, so we do not provide support for these integrations. The module essentially pulls data directly from the machine’s SQL server, meaning that as long as the machine is up and running with SQL properly configured, no additional setup is required on the machine side.  If you encounter any issues or need assistance with X-Link adapters, we recommend contacting eSupport@aiscorp.com. The AEGIS support team will assist you promptly.  Alternatively, if you are interested in IPC-CFX, this protocol is fully supported by AEGIS FactoryLogix and also includes complete support from Koh Young.  Please let me know if you have any further questions.   Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hello Paul,   ​   I'm sorry to hear you're facing this issue. Just to confirm, the exact problem is that the images are not being consistently generated, correct? Are the CSV files being generated properly, or are you experiencing issues with those as well?  Currently, images are saved through a GUI feature, this feature creates the image, and then BRM renames it according to your desired naming format and places it in the specified location.  If the image is not being saved correctly, the issue most likely occurs in the first part of the process (the GUI feature failing to save it), which would prevent BRM from renaming or relocating the file afterward.         Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hola JorgeAlberto,     Muchas gracias por el ticket. Con esto dejamos registro del cambio que necesitaban. Básicamente, lo que hicimos fue eliminar una condición en su código. Esta condición, al tener los interlockings habilitados y el Work Order des habilitado, evitaba que las piezas se procesaran. Supongo que esto se implementó originalmente con la intención de no inspeccionar piezas sin Work Order.  Ahora, con la nueva modalidad donde utilizan Work Order = -1, dicha condición ya no tiene sentido. Únicamente se comentó en el código, y aquí dejo el KBR de referencia en caso de que deseen aplicarlo en sus demás líneas.  De momento procederemos a cerrar este ticket ya que hicimos las pruebas en vivo en su equipo y funciono de acuerdo a lo esperado. Si necesitan soporte en el futuro, no duden en contactarnos nuevamente a nuestro correo oficial de soporte kya_support@kohyoung.com.     Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hola , Gabriel.    ¿Podría verificar la configuración del firewall?  tanto AOI como review  Asegúrese de que el firewall esté apagado. Además, siga los pasos a continuación para configurar los puertos BRM para AOI: Presione Win + R, escriba firewall.cpl y presione enter.        Haga clic en Configuración avanzada.         En el panel izquierdo, haga clic en Reglas de entrada, luego en el panel derecho, haga clic en Acciones > Nueva regla.        selecciona Puerto y da click en siguiente.        Elija TCP y escriba los puertos específicos "8020, 8021" y luego haga clic en Siguiente.        ​ Seleccione Permitir la conexión y luego haga clic en Siguiente.         Click siguiente     ​ Complete un nombre para la nueva regla (por ejemplo, Puerto BRM AOI) y haga clic en Finalizar para guardar la regla.          Después de crear la regla de entrada, repita los mismos pasos para las reglas de salida.      Avíseme una vez que esté completo o si necesita más ayuda o si esto resuelve el problema que tiene con los puertos.   Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: ​Hello, Hector.  I noticed that your site does not appear to use any custom development. I have attached an introduction to BRM, our MES software.      We currently have two standard solutions that we can provide free of charge, as long as this is your first development. I’m sharing some information about these solutions so that you can evaluate them and decide which one would be more appropriate for your infrastructure and requirements.     KYStandard 2.0: This solution generates inspection information using output files (XML or CSV). Please review the document "KSMART BRM (KY FileExport V2.0).pdf" for detailed information regarding the content and output format of these files.  KYStandard 3.0: Unlike the previous one, this development uses JSON messages to send information via Web Services to a server on your side (No files, only web service messages). Please review the document "KSMART BRM (KY Standard V3.0).pdf" to find a list of all the messages included in this development, along with the content of each message.     I’ll be awaiting your feedback once you have decided which solution works best for you. Once I receive that information, I will proceed to create and share the necessary files so you can complete the installation on your machines. If you have any questions or need clarification, feel free to reach out, and we can review them together.           Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hola Hello, John.  Before I begin explaining the output solutions we offer, let me first mention that our preferred connection method when integrating with ARCH Systems is through an industry standard called CFX. From my understanding, this is also their preferred connection method we’ve never connected to ARCH using file-based communication.  That said, by default the machine doesn’t generate an output file. This is because, in most cases, our clients prefer us to align with the specific output format they require, meaning each output is tailored to the customer’s needs. However, if that’s not the case, we also have standard output solutions available:    We currently have two standard solutions that we can provide free of charge, as long as this is your first development. I’ll be awaiting your feedback once you have decided which solution works best for you. docs.reply: Hola Jorge,     Es correcto: estos 10 extra parameters sí estaban considerados en su documento inicial y, hasta el día de hoy, los seguimos incluyendo en el mensaje. Sin embargo, no es como que quedaron como puntos pendientes para futuros requerimientos.  En el momento de la implementación (hace dos años), intentamos aclarar qué esperaban recibir en estos campos para asegurar que obtuvieran lo que necesitaban. Esta revisión se realizó con Reynaldo Barrera. En ese entonces, ustedes únicamente requerían un string vacío, y nosotros propusimos enviar información que consideramos que podría serles útil.  ​ 1   2    3   4       Originalmente ustedes buscaban solo un "String vació" y les propusimos mandar datos que pensamos serian de utilidad para ustedes.   La implementación que hicieron es la correcta, crear campos para desarrollos futuros lo cual es lo que esta sucediendo actualmente, es un nuevo desarrollo y por lo tanto tanto de su lado como de nosotros es necesario modificar el código original, de nuestro lado para enviar mas datos y de su lado para recibir estos nuevos datos.   Ahora que sabemos en donde están esperando recibir estos nuevos datos, entendemos que no es necesario una reestructuracion mayor al código por lo que se puede hacer el cambio a un costo menor $1500      Quedamos al pendiente de sus comentarios.     Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Qué tal Jorge Luis,   Parece que estamos combinando muchos issues en un mismo ticket:   Issue #1: Issue con el Xtra Params Este issue ya fue solucionado como comentó mi compañero Alexis. Lo que hacía falta era habilitar el checkbox de "Condition". Si utilizan el último KBR proporcionado por él, ya cuenta con esta opción seteada por default. Se puede tener una sesión para que se valide en tiempo real que estén recibiendo los datos.   Issue #2: Encontramos un código donde NO reportó SPI en todos los hijos En este issue se aprecia claramente que el problema es el servidor. Se intentó 3 veces hacer la conexión a su server y no hubo respuesta por parte de este. Para que quede mejor aclarado en qué parte está el issue, podemos colocar herramientas como Fiddler, que se encarga de registrar los paquetes que salen del equipo y los que llegan. Con esto se pudiera confirmar que el mensaje sale a su server pero no hay respuesta, para que lo puedan canalizar con su equipo de IT. docs.reply: Hello Kevin,     BRM is the software we use to enable the machines with file exports, MES connectivity, and, in this case, custom file-printing capabilities.   Using this software, we can implement a feature that automatically generates the HTML report and prints it after a board is classified in the review station, without requiring the user to open the file in a web browser. Please review the HTML format and contents in the SharePoint file, as implementing this format and this sequence would cost $1,500 USD.   The format exported by the review cannot be modified. If needed, we can use the BRM software to reproduce the current format and add the required fields. This reproduced format would then be printed. The cost for this development would be $4,000 USD.   Please let us know which implementation you are interested in so we can provide a formal quote.       Best regards

docs.reply: Hola Juan,     La secuencia para la inserción de resultados a sus bases de datos se realiza de la siguiente manera:  Al finalizar la inspección para tablillas con resultado GOOD o la clasificación en la Review Station, se insertan resultados a siixsem_aoi_ky_results para los campos:  serial, modelo, estatus, linea, herramienta_linea, atributo1, atributo2, atributo3, atributo4, atributo5, fecha_inicio_prueba, fecha_fin_prueba, pcbguid   El campo id_aoi se incrementa automáticamente y es manejado por la base de datos, no lo modificamos nosotros.  Para escribir resultados en las tablas de mediciones y dfectos, leemos el id_aoi asociado a la inspección y realizamos las inserciones correspondientes, como se acordó durante el desarrollo del feature.       Saludos cordiales
docs.cust: Luis, gracias por tu pronta respuesta. Más que un problema, queremos entender como es que funciona la inserción de los datos a las tablas donde se encuentran los resultados para saber como extraerlos...

docs.reply: Hola Juan,     Nosotros insertamos información en las tablas sql con nombres:   siixsem_aoi_ky_results  siixsem_aoi_defectos  siixsem_aoi_mediciones   Si pudieran indicarnos cuál de estas tablas presenta problemas y en qué campos, podríamos continuar con el análisis      Saludos cordiales
docs.cust: Buen día Ramiro, Agradezco el apoyo y entendemos bien lo que nos mencionas, sin embargo tenemos dos problemas: 1. Hay casos donde los registros en la tabla original no se trasladan a la tabla...

docs.reply: Hola Juan,     Lamentablemente, por políticas de la empresa no permitimos que sistemas de terceros accedan a nuestros servidores SQL debido a preocupaciones de integridad de datos y rendimiento. Sin embargo, ofrecemos varios métodos alternativos para acceder a esta información a través de interfaces estándar como IPC-CFX (recomendado), SECS/GEM, iTAC, Panasonic iLNB, ASYS Pulse, entre otros. También contamos con opciones no estándar como Web Services, Output Files, TCP/IP, conexiones SQL directas, y más. Otra opción es que nos redefinan los requerimientos de su herramienta actual de base de datos y la desarrollamos de acuerdo a sus necesidades.         Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hello Ian,  Sorry for the delayed response. I was working on the pin number implementation.  After reviewing the CFX documentation, the Measurement class is intended to contain only measurement related data.  Based on the official structure, for example Measurement and PCBInspection.OffsetMeasurement, it does not allow adding extra fields or custom information beyond the defined measurement parameters.  Because of this limitation, I cannot include additional pin level information directly inside the Measurement object without going against the CFX structure.        ​        ​  As a practical option, we could include the LeadID or Pin Number within the MeasurementName, for example:    Overhang_PIN_NUMBER ​   This would maintain compatibility with the CFX format while still providing the pin traceability needed for DPMO calculations.  Another option would be to include the LeadID directly in each reported defect entry, if that works better for your data processing.  Please let me know which option you prefer.    Best regards

docs.reply: Hi Adriana,    I've prepared an updated version of the UnitsInspected scripts with additional debug   logging. The purpose of these logs is to help us identify the root cause of the   TotalLeads:0 issue.    Our current theory is that most components (resistors, capacitors, etc.) only have a   "Body" type inspection in the coverage report — they don't have individual lead   inspections like connectors do. The script currently filters out Body-type entries   when counting leads, which would explain why only J1 (a connector with actual lead   inspections) shows a lead count while everything else returns 0.    The new version will log the actual contents of the coverage report so we can confirm    this. Specifically, it will capture:   - The column names in the coverage report   - The distinct component names (first 30)   - The distinct inspection types (Body, Lead1, Lead2, etc.)    You can download the updated KBR here: https://kohyoung-my.sharepoint.com/:f:/p/ramiro_rubio/IgA1CXcaqYUKQJj4bZ3fey3VASKT7Aa2A4LOwgUygkirlPc     Once you run an inspection with this version, please share the BRM logs so we can   analyze the data and determine the best fix.   Alexis Álvarez  MES Developer.  Koh Young America

docs.reply: Hello Nick,     Just a quick recap of the connection we had yesterday. We were able to remote into the machine and install the software; however, when we attempted to connect to the CFX server, we were unable to determine how to proceed. There appears to be a mismatch in expectations between what we (KY) anticipated regarding the CFX implementation and what FactoryLogix was expecting.     From my perspective, all CFX implementations I have seen since joining the company have been based on a set of well-defined CFX messages communicated via the AMQP protocol. These messages are published to a broker (typically a RabbitMQ server) and then processed by the end application.     In this case, it seems that instead of receiving standard CFX messages, KY was expected to deliver a file to a shared folder mapped to the PC. I am not sure whether this approach is even compatible with CFX (implementing CFX communication through files) however, we are open to aligning, provided that proper documentation is available. Specifically, we would need to know the file naming structure, the expected format (XML, CSV, JSON, etc.), and the exact content required in order to proceed with development.     My impression is that there may simply be a confusion in terminology (files vs. messages), and that we just need to align on what the CFX standard defines on its official website. Please let me know if you would prefer to set up a call with the three of us (TT Engineer / KohYoung / FactoryLogix Engineer) so we can clarify and resolve this together.     Saludos cordiales
docs.cust: Notes from todays install on the SPI. @Mark Raymer<mailto:mark.raymer@ttelectronics.com> please indicate how you want the files exported for Rabbit MQ. Regards,

docs.reply: Hello Mark,         Thanks for the clarification this makes total sense this will align to the CFX Standard completely. There are 3 things that Aegis needs to provide:  Machine Handle - How they need our machine to be named for them to parse the messages Example: KohYoung.Zenith.APSL000  Broker Host - The remote AMQP endpoint Example: amqp://cfx.aiscorp.com:5672  Publish Exchange - Location to send messages Example: /exchange/KohYoung     If they can provide these fields beforehand then we won't need them on the call. Please let me know your comments.      Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hello Felix,  To help clarify the situation, we can review both the GetUnitInfo and SaveResults messages directly from the BRM logs. These logs are automatically generated during normal operation, so they should provide accurate examples of the current behavior.  You can access them at the following path:  C:\KohYoung\KSmartBusinessRules\Log  Please let me know if you need support reviewing the log files or extracting the specific messages.  Best regards
docs.cust: I would like to know if there is a way for me to generate example files from the GetUnitInfo and SaveResults. GetUnitInfo in my mind should be easy, but the SaveResults, i'll need for the GetUnitInfo...

docs.reply: Hello, Felix.  You can open these .db files using the embedded SQLite viewer in BRM. Alternatively, you can download DB Browser for SQLite and open the .db files with that software.    ​In BRM, go to Log Viewer.       ​A new window will be displayed. Click on Add Database.      In the next window, select the .db file you want to view and click Open.              ​Once the database is added, double click on it, then double click on the log table and select Data to view all the logs.       ​    ​  Regarding your question: “Do the files contain GetUnitInfo and TestResult even if the FlexFlow function is disabled?” ​   No, those messages are only generated when the machine is running with the FlexFlow feature enabled.    Saludos cordiales

De acuerdo, adicionalmente les recomiendo instalar y dejar ejecutando en segundo plano esta aplicación adjunta en el correo ,   Fiddler Classic es una herramienta de diagnóstico que nos permite revisar la comunicación entre la máquina  y los servidores con los que se conecta.  Su función principal es capturar y mostrar los mensajes que se envían y reciben (como solicitudes y respuestas), lo que nos ayuda a identificar problemas de comunicación, retrasos, errores o información incorrecta que pueda estar afectando.  Con esta herramienta podemos analizar de forma más precisa la causa raíz del  problema y validar si la información se está enviando y recibiendo correctamente   Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hello jswebest Team,       The machine interface requires development, based on your requirements here are our comments:     The web service interface can be implemented using the following commands: Device Status (POST), Get X-Board (GET), and Send X-Board (POST).  Using the defined web messages, the machine can POST a device status message when an error occurs. After a barcode is read, the Get X-Board interface can be called, and once the inspection is completed, the Send X-Board message can be transmitted.     In the Log file, ​not every type of inspection contains the Windows details or the CompAngle. for these values is an empty string or a null value expected?       Foxconn Team,   Are there interlocks required to stop the machine depending on the response obtained from the web service interactions? Please let us know if you are interested in this development so we can share a formal quote.     Best regards

docs.reply: Hola Irene,     Muchas gracias por el video. En el escenario que se muestra, se cae en un caso especial. Déjame explicarte cómo funciona actualmente el proceso de autojuzgado.    La PCB se inspecciona en AOI.    Al término de la inspección, verificamos si alguno de los defectos identificados por la AOI debe ser autoclasificado.    Los defectos se autoclasifican en base a las reglas configuradas.    Se abre la review y se carga la PCB.    Si se selecciona un defecto que fue autojuzgado, ocurre lo siguiente: 5.1. La pantalla se bloquea (no permite reclasificar). 5.2. Aparece el mensaje en pantalla: “Please judge PCB”. 5.3. Se avanza automáticamente al siguiente defecto.    Si se selecciona un defecto que no está autoclasificado, el sistema permite operar de manera normal.    En el video, el problema es que solo existe un defecto y es de los auto juzgados. Para que el juzgado se complete en la review, el operador debe realizar el clic final en la tablilla (Pass o NG). En algún punto es necesario desbloquear la pantalla para que el operador pueda realizar este paso final.  Actualmente no es posible desactivar únicamente el botón de Pass; solo se puede activar o desactivar todo el tablero. Como puedes ver, todo el tablero se pone en gris cuando se selecciona un defecto auto clasificado, pero al tratarse de un único defecto, es necesario desbloquearlo para poder concluir la operación.     ​Se puede hacer algunas cosas para mitigar este problema:     1- Cuando sea una pieza de 1 solo defecto (y este defecto este en las reglas de autojuzgado) se pede que en automatico poner la pieza en NG (Si hacemos esto ni siquiera apareceria en la review como pending board)   2- Cuando sea una pieza de 1 solo defecto (y este defecto este en las reglas de autojuzgado) se puede que despleguemos un mensaje en pantalla con alguna leyenda "Pieza con 1 solo defecto, favor de seleccionar NG al desbloquearse el tablero"  ​   Quedo al pendiente de sus comentarios.       Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hola Victor,  El día de hoy realizamos pruebas con RabbitMQ, que es el broker de mensajería necesario para la comunicación CFX. Instalamos el broker en una máquina conectada directamente al switch de la AOI y logramos establecer comunicación exitosamente.  Sin embargo, al replicar esta misma configuración utilizando una computadora remota que se encuentra detrás de la infraestructura de IT (firewalls, etc.), la comunicación falló.  Quedamos atentos a los comentarios del equipo de IT para que habiliten los puertos y reglas necesarias.   ​     Saludos cordiales

docs.reply: Hola Alexander,     Muchísimas gracias por compartir que sí lo han intentado. Ahora que sabemos cuál es el comportamiento que están experimentando, les podemos dar explicación y sugerencias sobre qué hacer.   La razón por la que está tardando demasiado es la siguiente: Anteriormente había issues que reportaban aproximadamente 20 días después de que sucedieran. Como el número de días default para guardar logs es de 14 días, les sugerimos aumentar este número para que tuviéramos acceso a los logs anteriores. Hasta donde tengo entendido, tienen 30 días, por lo que es el doble de información y, por ende, tarda tanto en generarlo. Si no desean que tarde demasiado, favor de regresarlo a 14 días; sin embargo, consideren que solo tendrá visibilidad de los logs de los últimos 14 días.        Ahora, para recuperar los logs de este evento en particular, te sugiero borrar la carpeta de logs para empezar en blanco y dejar correr tal vez 1 día para que el evento que reportan se presente. Una vez suceda el error, intenten generar el issue report y no debería tomar mucho tiempo. Quedo al pendiente de sus comentarios. Es importante mantener la comunicación para saber qué comportamiento es el que están teniendo y poder darles sugerencias de acuerdo a lo visto.      Saludos cordiales
